{"id":"brutus-7089","title":"Add Tier-Limits-Exit line to winner headers in PnL PDF","notes":"User request:
In /Users/gc/gitrepos/brutus/model/data/results/rerender-20260216174947/pnl-260216-175252.pdf, each table already shows a second line in the "Target / Tier-Limits-Exit" column (example: "FAST - T4 L3 S3 - kelly_0.25"). Add that same second-line detail to the two winner header sections.

Required behavior:
1) Long Winner section near the top:
- Keep the existing first line: "Long Winner: <target_name>"
- Add second line directly below it with tier/limits/exit text
- Then continue with existing Mean Return / CV / Contest Score line

2) Short Winner section further down:
- Same pattern as Long Winner

For this specific sample PDF, expected second lines are:
- Long winner: "FAST - T4 L3 S3 - vwap_rank"
- Short winner: "FAST - T4 L3 S3 - baseline"

Code locations (primary):
- /Users/gc/gitrepos/brutus/model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/output.py:607
  winner header currently rendered in _write_pdf() as one line only
- Reuse existing formatter in same file:
  - _format_pdf_tier_limits_exit_label(...):1028
  - _format_pdf_target_label(...):1043

Implementation guidance:
- Use winner-level fields: result.params_tier and result.exit_algorithm
- Render second line only when there is tier and/or exit algorithm data
- Place second line immediately between winner line and Mean Return line
- Keep existing table rendering unchanged
- Keep CSV outputs unchanged

Testing updates required:
- File: /Users/gc/gitrepos/brutus/model/tests/pipeline/test_pnl_roll_output.py
- Add/extend PDF test to verify both winner blocks include second-line tier/limits/exit text in order, not just anywhere in the document.
- Recommended assertion style: regex/order checks over extracted PDF text, e.g.
  - Long: "Long Winner: <target> ... FAST - T4 L3 S3 - <algo> ... Mean Return:"
  - Short: "Short Winner: <target> ... FAST - T4 L3 S3 - <algo> ... Mean Return:"
- Ensure the test fails if the string appears only in table rows.

Verification (mandatory for Python/model changes):
- make model-sanity

new_value: {"notes":"Find offending commits from last 24h, identify high-memory paths in phases 4/5, and propose remediation plan.\nHandoff capsule (investigation only):\\n- Identified likely regressions in combined round path introduced in 5c65dae (holds long+short SideRoundState/lazy_cache concurrently; round_robin_finalize.py:214-337).\\n- Identified memory amplifier in 7d43bcd: CombinedFinalist now copies all_algo_period_returns for every finalist candidate conversion (combined_round_contract.py:154-175, 268-271).\\n- Identified additional amplifier in 1789e41: cross-product now always emits two strategies and dedupe no longer collapses different training windows (combined_round_contract.py:288-373), increasing candidate count and scoring workload.\\n- Found evaluator materializes combined_returns for all candidates in memory before winner selection (combined_round_evaluator.py:143-190, 303-311).\\n\\nWhat remains:\\n- Reproduce with RSS instrumentation to quantify stage peaks (long R1-R3, short R1-R3, finalist conversion, candidate scoring).\\n- Implement fast rollback/flag path to legacy per-side R4/R5 for immediate mitigation.\\n- Implement memory-safe finalist selection (rank RoundResult first, convert only top_n finalists).\\n- Implement streaming candidate scoring to avoid retaining combined_returns for all candidates.\\n- Re-test with production-like thread settings and candidate cardinality.\\n\\nVerification steps:\\n- For any Python/model code changes: make model-sanity\\n"}

{"id":"brutus-g4my","title":"Merge long and short training","description":"Today we run contests for long and short separately which is a procedural flaw.  We are running pnl strategies on only one side at a time.  In the real world we are going to be trading long and shorts from the same account.

Therefore our training sweep / round-robin must construct it's cartesian product of 

- long model params
- long model target params
- short model params
- short model target params
- short model ticker selection params
- exit strategy

and execute all the variations.  note the difference - by grouping the long and short variations together, we can apply one exit strategy at a time to the whole portfolio (just like in real life)

note that this will probably impact strategies as well, which may not be well supporting handling longs and shorts at the same time.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-15T16:18:32Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-17T09:32:18Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"Handoff (memory forward-fix, no selection logic changes)

What changed
- Added RSS observability helper at model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/memory_observability.py and wired logs through combined round finalize/evaluator/orchestrator paths.
- Reduced finalist materialization memory in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/combined_round_contract.py by sorting RoundResult first and converting only top_n finalists (ordering/selection semantics preserved).
- Reduced candidate scoring retention in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/combined_round_evaluator.py by not storing per-candidate combined_returns payloads; recompute combined_returns only for winner.
- Added cache-trim and restore helpers in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_finalize.py + model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/combined_round_orchestrator.py; release per-side cache entries as soon as no longer needed.
- Added SideRoundState metadata in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_types.py + model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_staged.py to preserve selected-feature rebuild behavior when restoring winner cache entry.
- Updated tests: model/tests/pipeline/test_combined_round_evaluator.py and model/tests/pipeline/test_combined_round_integration.py.

What remains
- Validate RSS behavior under the real remote workload (c7gd.12xlarge, 2 competition threads, 12 fitting threads) and confirm OOM is eliminated.
- If RSS still peaks too high, remove duplicated side source matrices by sharing/releasing _LazyCacheBuilder source state across long/short combined path without changing winner selection semantics.
- Capture before/after RSS checkpoints from new logs around: before/after long R1-R3, before/after short R1-R3, candidate scoring progress, pre-final-training, and post-side-cache-release.
- Once stable, commit and attach commit/pr labels then stage verification.

Verification steps
- make model-sanity
"}

{"id":"brutus-g4my","title":"Merge long and short training","description":"Today we run contests for long and short separately which is a procedural flaw.  We are running pnl strategies on only one side at a time.  In the real world we are going to be trading long and shorts from the same account.

Therefore our training sweep / round-robin must construct it's cartesian product of 

- long model params
- long model target params
- short model params
- short model target params
- short model ticker selection params
- exit strategy

and execute all the variations.  note the difference - by grouping the long and short variations together, we can apply one exit strategy at a time to the whole portfolio (just like in real life)

note that this will probably impact strategies as well, which may not be well supporting handling longs and shorts at the same time.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-15T16:18:32Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-17T10:36:39Z"}

Progress update:\n- Created workflow tasks: 1brutus-a0t.1 (Workflow A local), 1brutus-a0t.2 (Workflow A docs), 1brutus-a0t.5 (Workflow B remote), 1brutus-a0t.6 (Workflow B docs).\n- Closed older validation-chain tasks as superseded by this epic workflow approach: 1brutus-pn6, 1brutus-maf, 1brutus-u2e, 1brutus-kn1, 1brutus-cmm.\n- Left 1brutus-4yw untouched per user request to ignore it for now.\n- Workflow A implementation is complete in code and awaiting manual user validation before proceeding to Workflow B.

HANDOFF CAPSULE (formatted)

Context
- Reopened this bead to continue yesterday-vs-today divergence analysis.
- Side detour: 1brutus-6e7g tracks reporting cleanup for long signal pct/rate naming.

Established facts
- Compared runs:
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260210052705
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260211053848
- Winners changed:
  - long: target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d -> target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d
  - short: target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d -> target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d
- short_normalize_gap=false in both runs and is post-sweep selection logic; not the cause of 90->80 winner flip.
- pnl meta long_signals (327 -> 205) is sweep/PnL signal_count, not test-label positives.
- Test-label positives for winning long target are:
  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%
  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%
- Equal test trading days (212) is due to architecture:
  - one global date split is created from full features after removing 4 gap + 1 holdout day
  - split5 validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df
  - winners are evaluated/persisted on that shared window
- Reconstructed split5/test window for the later snapshot:
  - test: 2025-04-01 .. 2026-02-03 (212 dates)
  - gap: 2026-02-04 .. 2026-02-09
  - holdout: 2026-02-10
- Staged sweep folds are:
  - R1=1, R2=1, R3=2, R4=5, R5(extra groups)=5
  - no 3-fold final round in current sweep path
- Sweep-path training phase mostly reuses sweep-trained winners rather than retraining.

Additional reconstruction
- Replayed winning long execution path (vwap_rank) for projected entries:
  - run-20260210052705: proba>0.5 candidates=52, long_positions_taken=39
  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates=76, long_positions_taken=57
- Confirms distinction needed between:
  - long_signals (label opportunities)
  - long_positions (entered positions under execution constraints)

Hypotheses for divergence
- H1: One-day snapshot shift (2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10) changed fold boundaries/composition enough to flip close contests.
- H2: Config/algorithm drift (notably kelly in later run config/sweep) altered elimination dynamics and winner choice.
- H3: Reporting conflation (meta signal_count vs label positives) made changes appear impossible when comparing unlike metrics.

What remains
- Controlled A/B reruns:
  1) same snapshot, kelly off/on
  2) same algorithms, snapshot +1 day
  3) compare round histories/winner flips
- Reporting standardization across Slack/console/PDF/CSV:
  - long_signal_pct = positives / total_test_rows
  - long_signal_rate = positives / total_test_trading_days
  - keep sweep signal_count under separate label
- Lock naming for “rate” units (per-day count vs percent-per-day).

Verification
- Investigation only so far; no code changes.
- For future Python/model edits in this bead, verify only with: make model-sanity
CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, post-exit-validity test)

New findings
- Re-tested the concern that PnL candidates were low because exits failed to compute.
- For both winning long runs, exit validity is 100% once a row is admitted as a candidate:
  - run-20260210052705: signals(proba>0.5)=52, baseline valid exits=52/52, stoploss=52/52, vwap=52/52
  - run-20260211053848: signals(proba>0.5)=76, baseline valid exits=76/76, stoploss=76/76, vwap=76/76
- Therefore, low candidate counts are NOT due to missing forward bars / missing entry / invalid exit rows in these runs.

Where the drop actually occurs
- Candidate gate for long classifier in sweep is hard-coded at predict_proba > 0.5.
- Label positives (TP+FN) are ~2.9k, but proba>0.5 rows are 52/76.
- This indicates a probability-thresholding/calibration mismatch rather than horizon-data completeness.

Sanity checks performed
- Not a class-direction inversion:
  - AUC(p) ~ 0.60/0.62
  - AUC(1-p) ~ 0.40/0.38
- So p is in the correct direction; values are just concentrated low.

Next step (in progress)
- Isolate raw base-model scores vs calibrated probabilities in sweep-trained winner artifacts to determine whether calibration compresses probabilities and suppresses candidate volume.

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, raw vs calibrated probabilities)

Raw-vs-calibrated analysis on saved winner models
- Loaded sweep winner artifacts and compared base model predict_proba vs calibrated model predict_proba on exact persisted test rows.
- Stored position_signals predict_proba matches calibrated output exactly (max abs diff ~1e-16).

Run-level results
- run-20260210052705 (long target from_2023):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 287
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 52
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6022)
  - calibration strongly reshapes probability scale and suppresses >0.5 candidates.
- run-20260211053848 (long target from_2021):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 90
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 76
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6232)
  - calibration effect on >0.5 count is smaller.

Implication
- Candidate collapse is primarily a probability-scale/calibration-threshold issue, not missing exit data.
- Current sweep candidate gate (proba > 0.5) is brittle across runs because calibration can change score scale while preserving ranking/AUC.

Likely bug class
- Metric/selection logic assumes a fixed semantic meaning for 0.5 after post-hoc calibration; this may be invalid for this problem and calibration procedure.

Linked under epic 1brutus-xu0d as active investigation/root-cause stream for implementation decisions.
### Handoff capsule (Kelly impact + signal/candidate findings)

What changed in this checkpoint
- Added a round-by-round comparison of yesterday vs today showing where Kelly changed algorithm rankings and elimination paths.
- Re-validated the long signal/candidate pipeline assumptions with concrete counts from persisted artifacts.

Key findings
- Shared test window behavior is confirmed (same test dates within a run across target start dates); differing start dates mainly change training history, not the sweep test fold.
- Long label-positive prevalence for winning targets is stable around ~19% of test rows (TP+FN from `global_metrics.json`), not ~3%.
- Low `pnl` candidate volume is not caused by missing exit computations:
  - Yesterday winner long: candidates 52 -> valid exits 52
  - Today winner long: candidates 76 -> valid exits 76
- Candidate collapse is consistent with fixed `predict_proba > 0.5` on calibrated probabilities:
  - Yesterday long winner: raw model >0.5 = 287, calibrated >0.5 = 52
  - Today long winner: raw model >0.5 = 90, calibrated >0.5 = 76
  - AUC raw vs calibrated unchanged in both runs (ranking preserved, scale shifted).

Kelly impact, round by round (examples)
- Long Round 1 (`from_2023` head-to-head):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d` with `vwap_rank`, contest_score `-0.551170` (rank 1).
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, contest_score `-0.012841` (rank 1).
- Long Round 2 (`from_2021` survivor):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d` with `stoploss`, score `-0.024692`.
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, score `-0.002590`.
- Long Round 3 (final long target duel, both runs using `vwap_rank`):
  - Yesterday winner: `...from_2023...` score `0.184970` vs `...from_2021...` score `-0.033495`.
  - Today winner: `...from_2021...` score `0.079508` vs `...from_2023...` score `0.027562`.
  - Interpretation: Kelly materially changed earlier-round algorithm competitiveness; final flip at R3 is also influenced by day+1 data drift.
- Short Round 1 illustrates broader reshuffling:
  - Yesterday top rows were mostly baseline/stoploss/vwap.
  - Today top contenders include Kelly variants (`kelly_0.5`, `kelly_0.25`) with different target survival order.

Hypotheses (most likely first)
1. Fixed post-calibration gate (`>0.5`) is too brittle; calibration scale drift can drastically alter tradable candidate volume without hurting rank quality.
2. Kelly introduction changed early-round contest scores and elimination paths, which changes which targets reach final rounds.
3. One extra market day plus short-threshold config change (90 -> 80) further perturbed rankings.

What remains
- Implement configurable long candidate gating policy (`1brutus-xu0d.1`) and wire metrics so we can compare candidate coverage under alternative gates.
- Run controlled A/B on yesterday/today artifacts (`1brutus-xu0d.2`) to isolate Kelly-vs-gate-vs-data effects.
- Add regression coverage for `long_signal_pct`, per-day signal rate, candidate counts, and projected position-open rate (`1brutus-xu0d.3`).

Verification steps
- Investigation-only checkpoint (no code edits in this update).
- For upcoming Python/model changes, verification target remains: `make model-sanity`.
CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-12, sweep long gate policy wired)

What changed
- Implemented configurable long candidate gating in sweep PnL evaluation by replacing hard-coded `predict_proba > 0.5` with a resolved threshold policy in:
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/sweep_evaluator.py
- Added `long_prediction_threshold` wiring to sweep-time evaluator construction:
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/sweep_phase.py
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_executor.py
- Added focused regression coverage in:
  - model/tests/pipeline/test_pnl_roll_evaluator.py
  - verifies configured long threshold is honored
  - verifies non-long classifier path keeps legacy 0.5
  - verifies invalid threshold values fail fast

Behavioral outcome
- Sweep long classifier candidate generation is now policy-driven (`auto` adaptive valley or numeric threshold), not fixed at 0.5.
- Legacy fixed behavior remains available by setting `long_prediction_threshold = 0.5`.

What remains
- A/B evaluate yesterday vs today runs under explicit threshold settings (xu0d.2) to quantify candidate-volume and winner-path deltas.
- Complete reporting metric alignment workstream (6e7g): long_signal_pct vs long_signal_rate labels across Slack/console/PDF/CSV.
- Add broader regression coverage for signal/candidate/position metrics (xu0d.3).

Verification
- make model-sanity
  - passed (lint, format-check, mypy, core tests)

user requested label as rate)\\n\\nScope:\\n- Ensure consistent naming and calculation source in all report surfaces\\n- Avoid conflating with pnl sweep signal_count metric in meta CSV\\n- Confirm winning-target-only semantics\nLinked under epic 1brutus-xu0d as reporting-surface workstream for long_signal_pct and long_signal_rate labeling/consistency.\nPlanning update (2026-02-12): added child P1 tasks 6e7g.1-6e7g.4 to track long+short inference and actionable-position metrics across sweep reporting surfaces (CSV/PDF/meta and downstream consumers).\\n\\nKey implementation research:\\n- Current sweep artifacts come from pnl_roll/output.py (summary, rounds, windows, meta, PDF).\\n- Current meta long_signals/short_signals are winner-level signal_count only, not per target/strategy coverage metrics.\\n- Long sweep path is classifier-based with thresholded predict_proba; {"id":"brutus-xu0d","title":"Fix long candidate gating and coverage reporting","description":"Implement and validate a robust long candidate selection policy so sweep/live candidate volume is not dictated by a brittle fixed probability cutoff, and make reporting metrics unambiguous across Slack/console/PDF/CSV.","notes":"Context
- Investigation in 1brutus-0qbw found low long candidate counts are caused by hard gate proba>0.5 interacting with calibration scale shifts, not missing exit data.

Goals
- Replace brittle fixed gate logic with an explicit operating-point policy.
- Preserve ranking signal while improving actionable candidate/position coverage.
- Publish clear, non-overloaded metrics: label opportunities vs model candidates vs executed positions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:01:43Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-17T09:32:19Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T19:42:33Z","close_reason":"All children shipped: xu0d.1 (PR #826), xu0d.2 (#830), xu0d.3 (#831), 0qbw (#828), 6e7g (#829)"}

{"id":"brutus-0qbw","title":"Scene poitier-inception: Compare unified outputs (yesterday vs today)","notes":"Investigate short/long target signal count differences in model/data/unified between yesterday and today runs.
Comparison complete for remote result runs run-20260210052705 (2026-02-10) and run-20260211053848 (2026-02-11).

Findings:
- Results download location during aws:model:train-remote is relative to task cwd model; default path resolves to model/model/data/results/run-id.
- Yesterday winner targets: long=target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d, short=target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d.
- Today winner targets: long=target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d, short=target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d.
- Meta signal counts changed: long 327 to 205, short 321 to 324, total 648 to 529.
- short_normalize_gap is false in both manifest snapshots manifest_20260210_055157.toml and manifest_20260211_063845.toml, so short 90 to 80 flip is not caused by normalize-gap toggle.
- Code path confirms Gate A min_gap and normalize_gap is evaluated after short target winner is selected, so it cannot decide 90 vs 80 target winner.

What remains:
- Optional follow-up: make orchestrate default pull path absolute so it lands at repo-root model/data/results even when task runs with dir model.
- Optional follow-up: persist manifest used for each remote run into results directory for reproducibility.

Verification steps:
- N/A investigation only no code changes.
HANDOFF CAPSULE (2026-02-11)\n\nContext\n- Reopened this bead to continue the yesterday-vs-today result divergence investigation.\n- Side detour was opened as 1brutus-6e7g to track metric/reporting cleanup (long signal pct/rate labels).\n\nWhat changed / established facts\n- Compared runs:\n  - yesterday: /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260210052705\n  - today: /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260211053848\n- Winner changes confirmed:\n  - long: target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d -> target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d\n  - short: target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d -> target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d\n- short_normalize_gap is FALSE in both relevant manifest snapshots; this is a post-sweep short selection setting and is not the cause of 90->80 target winner change.\n- The reported pnl meta field long_signals (327 -> 205) is NOT test-label positives; it is sweep/PnL signal_count from evaluator paths.\n- Test-label positives for winning long target (TP+FN from global_metrics) are stable/slightly higher, not lower:\n  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%\n  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%\n- Equal test trading days (212) explained by split architecture:\n  - sweep builds one global date split from full features (after 4 gap + 1 holdout day)\n  - last split validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df\n  - all winners are later evaluated/persisted on that shared test window\n  - result: roughly constant test-day count across start-date targets within a run.\n- Global split windows (run with 2026-02-10 latest date) were explicitly reconstructed:\n  - split5 val = 2025-04-01 .. 2026-02-03 (212 dates)\n  - gap = 2026-02-04 .. 2026-02-09\n  - holdout = 2026-02-10\n- Sweep stages/folds clarified from code:\n  - R1=1 fold, R2=1, R3=2, R4=5, R5(extra groups)=5\n  - no 3-fold final round in current staged sweep path.\n- In sweep path, training phase mostly reuses sweep-trained winners (wrapper/packaging behavior), not fresh fitting.\n\nAdditional metric reconstruction done\n- Replayed winning long execution path (vwap_rank) on test-set signals to estimate positions opened:\n  - run-20260210052705: proba>0.5 candidates 52, long_positions_taken 39\n  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates 76, long_positions_taken 57\n- This supports the desired metric distinction:\n  - long_signals (label opportunities) vs long_positions (actually entered).\n\nMain hypotheses for the divergence\n- H1: Input dataset advanced by one market day (features snapshot 2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10), shifting split boundaries and fold composition enough to flip marginal contest outcomes.\n- H2: Manifest/algorithm set drift between runs (notably kelly present in later run config/sweep) changed elimination dynamics and winner selection.\n- H3: Metric naming/reporting conflation (meta long_signals vs label positives) made the change appear mathematically impossible when it was actually comparing unlike quantities.\n\nWhat remains\n- Build controlled A/B reruns to isolate causes:\n  1) same features snapshot, toggle kelly off/on\n  2) same algorithms, shift features snapshot by one day\n  3) compare resulting round histories and winner flips\n- Standardize reporting outputs (Slack/console/PDF/CSV) to explicitly show:\n  - long_signal_pct = positives / total_test_rows\n  - long_signal_rate = positives / total_test_trading_days\n  - keep sweep signal_count under a separate label to avoid ambiguity\n- Decide final naming units for rate (per-day count vs percent-per-day) and lock labels.\n\nVerification\n- Investigation-only so far; no code changes committed.\n- For any upcoming Python/model code changes in this bead, verify ONLY with: make model-sanity\n
HANDOFF CAPSULE (formatted)

Context
- Reopened this bead to continue yesterday-vs-today divergence analysis.
- Side detour: 1brutus-6e7g tracks reporting cleanup for long signal pct/rate naming.

Established facts
- Compared runs:
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260210052705
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260211053848
- Winners changed:
  - long: target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d -> target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d
  - short: target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d -> target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d
- short_normalize_gap=false in both runs and is post-sweep selection logic; not the cause of 90->80 winner flip.
- pnl meta long_signals (327 -> 205) is sweep/PnL signal_count, not test-label positives.
- Test-label positives for winning long target are:
  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%
  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%
- Equal test trading days (212) is due to architecture:
  - one global date split is created from full features after removing 4 gap + 1 holdout day
  - split5 validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df
  - winners are evaluated/persisted on that shared window
- Reconstructed split5/test window for the later snapshot:
  - test: 2025-04-01 .. 2026-02-03 (212 dates)
  - gap: 2026-02-04 .. 2026-02-09
  - holdout: 2026-02-10
- Staged sweep folds are:
  - R1=1, R2=1, R3=2, R4=5, R5(extra groups)=5
  - no 3-fold final round in current sweep path
- Sweep-path training phase mostly reuses sweep-trained winners rather than retraining.

Additional reconstruction
- Replayed winning long execution path (vwap_rank) for projected entries:
  - run-20260210052705: proba>0.5 candidates=52, long_positions_taken=39
  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates=76, long_positions_taken=57
- Confirms distinction needed between:
  - long_signals (label opportunities)
  - long_positions (entered positions under execution constraints)

Hypotheses for divergence
- H1: One-day snapshot shift (2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10) changed fold boundaries/composition enough to flip close contests.
- H2: Config/algorithm drift (notably kelly in later run config/sweep) altered elimination dynamics and winner choice.
- H3: Reporting conflation (meta signal_count vs label positives) made changes appear impossible when comparing unlike metrics.

What remains
- Controlled A/B reruns:
  1) same snapshot, kelly off/on
  2) same algorithms, snapshot +1 day
  3) compare round histories/winner flips
- Reporting standardization across Slack/console/PDF/CSV:
  - long_signal_pct = positives / total_test_rows
  - long_signal_rate = positives / total_test_trading_days
  - keep sweep signal_count under separate label
- Lock naming for “rate” units (per-day count vs percent-per-day).

Verification
- Investigation only so far; no code changes.
- For future Python/model edits in this bead, verify only with: make model-sanity
CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, post-exit-validity test)

New findings
- Re-tested the concern that PnL candidates were low because exits failed to compute.
- For both winning long runs, exit validity is 100% once a row is admitted as a candidate:
  - run-20260210052705: signals(proba>0.5)=52, baseline valid exits=52/52, stoploss=52/52, vwap=52/52
  - run-20260211053848: signals(proba>0.5)=76, baseline valid exits=76/76, stoploss=76/76, vwap=76/76
- Therefore, low candidate counts are NOT due to missing forward bars / missing entry / invalid exit rows in these runs.

Where the drop actually occurs
- Candidate gate for long classifier in sweep is hard-coded at predict_proba > 0.5.
- Label positives (TP+FN) are ~2.9k, but proba>0.5 rows are 52/76.
- This indicates a probability-thresholding/calibration mismatch rather than horizon-data completeness.

Sanity checks performed
- Not a class-direction inversion:
  - AUC(p) ~ 0.60/0.62
  - AUC(1-p) ~ 0.40/0.38
- So p is in the correct direction; values are just concentrated low.

Next step (in progress)
- Isolate raw base-model scores vs calibrated probabilities in sweep-trained winner artifacts to determine whether calibration compresses probabilities and suppresses candidate volume.

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, raw vs calibrated probabilities)

Raw-vs-calibrated analysis on saved winner models
- Loaded sweep winner artifacts and compared base model predict_proba vs calibrated model predict_proba on exact persisted test rows.
- Stored position_signals predict_proba matches calibrated output exactly (max abs diff ~1e-16).

Run-level results
- run-20260210052705 (long target from_2023):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 287
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 52
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6022)
  - calibration strongly reshapes probability scale and suppresses >0.5 candidates.
- run-20260211053848 (long target from_2021):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 90
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 76
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6232)
  - calibration effect on >0.5 count is smaller.

Implication
- Candidate collapse is primarily a probability-scale/calibration-threshold issue, not missing exit data.
- Current sweep candidate gate (proba > 0.5) is brittle across runs because calibration can change score scale while preserving ranking/AUC.

Likely bug class
- Metric/selection logic assumes a fixed semantic meaning for 0.5 after post-hoc calibration; this may be invalid for this problem and calibration procedure.

Linked under epic 1brutus-xu0d as active investigation/root-cause stream for implementation decisions.
### Handoff capsule (Kelly impact + signal/candidate findings)

What changed in this checkpoint
- Added a round-by-round comparison of yesterday vs today showing where Kelly changed algorithm rankings and elimination paths.
- Re-validated the long signal/candidate pipeline assumptions with concrete counts from persisted artifacts.

Key findings
- Shared test window behavior is confirmed (same test dates within a run across target start dates); differing start dates mainly change training history, not the sweep test fold.
- Long label-positive prevalence for winning targets is stable around ~19% of test rows (TP+FN from `global_metrics.json`), not ~3%.
- Low `pnl` candidate volume is not caused by missing exit computations:
  - Yesterday winner long: candidates 52 -> valid exits 52
  - Today winner long: candidates 76 -> valid exits 76
- Candidate collapse is consistent with fixed `predict_proba > 0.5` on calibrated probabilities:
  - Yesterday long winner: raw model >0.5 = 287, calibrated >0.5 = 52
  - Today long winner: raw model >0.5 = 90, calibrated >0.5 = 76
  - AUC raw vs calibrated unchanged in both runs (ranking preserved, scale shifted).

Kelly impact, round by round (examples)
- Long Round 1 (`from_2023` head-to-head):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d` with `vwap_rank`, contest_score `-0.551170` (rank 1).
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, contest_score `-0.012841` (rank 1).
- Long Round 2 (`from_2021` survivor):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d` with `stoploss`, score `-0.024692`.
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, score `-0.002590`.
- Long Round 3 (final long target duel, both runs using `vwap_rank`):
  - Yesterday winner: `...from_2023...` score `0.184970` vs `...from_2021...` score `-0.033495`.
  - Today winner: `...from_2021...` score `0.079508` vs `...from_2023...` score `0.027562`.
  - Interpretation: Kelly materially changed earlier-round algorithm competitiveness; final flip at R3 is also influenced by day+1 data drift.
- Short Round 1 illustrates broader reshuffling:
  - Yesterday top rows were mostly baseline/stoploss/vwap.
  - Today top contenders include Kelly variants (`kelly_0.5`, `kelly_0.25`) with different target survival order.

Hypotheses (most likely first)
1. Fixed post-calibration gate (`>0.5`) is too brittle; calibration scale drift can drastically alter tradable candidate volume without hurting rank quality.
2. Kelly introduction changed early-round contest scores and elimination paths, which changes which targets reach final rounds.
3. One extra market day plus short-threshold config change (90 -> 80) further perturbed rankings.

What remains
- Implement configurable long candidate gating policy (`1brutus-xu0d.1`) and wire metrics so we can compare candidate coverage under alternative gates.
- Run controlled A/B on yesterday/today artifacts (`1brutus-xu0d.2`) to isolate Kelly-vs-gate-vs-data effects.
- Add regression coverage for `long_signal_pct`, per-day signal rate, candidate counts, and projected position-open rate (`1brutus-xu0d.3`).

Verification steps
- Investigation-only checkpoint (no code edits in this update).
- For upcoming Python/model changes, verification target remains: `make model-sanity`.
CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-12, sweep long gate policy wired)

What changed
- Implemented configurable long candidate gating in sweep PnL evaluation by replacing hard-coded `predict_proba > 0.5` with a resolved threshold policy in:
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/sweep_evaluator.py
- Added `long_prediction_threshold` wiring to sweep-time evaluator construction:
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/sweep_phase.py
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_executor.py
- Added focused regression coverage in:
  - model/tests/pipeline/test_pnl_roll_evaluator.py
  - verifies configured long threshold is honored
  - verifies non-long classifier path keeps legacy 0.5
  - verifies invalid threshold values fail fast

Behavioral outcome
- Sweep long classifier candidate generation is now policy-driven (`auto` adaptive valley or numeric threshold), not fixed at 0.5.
- Legacy fixed behavior remains available by setting `long_prediction_threshold = 0.5`.

What remains
- A/B evaluate yesterday vs today runs under explicit threshold settings (xu0d.2) to quantify candidate-volume and winner-path deltas.
- Complete reporting metric alignment workstream (6e7g): long_signal_pct vs long_signal_rate labels across Slack/console/PDF/CSV.
- Add broader regression coverage for signal/candidate/position metrics (xu0d.3).

Verification
- make model-sanity
  - passed (lint, format-check, mypy, core tests)

HANDOFF CAPSULE (formatted)

Context
- Reopened this bead to continue yesterday-vs-today divergence analysis.
- Side detour: 1brutus-6e7g tracks reporting cleanup for long signal pct/rate naming.

Established facts
- Compared runs:
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260210052705
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260211053848
- Winners changed:
  - long: target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d -> target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d
  - short: target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d -> target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d
- short_normalize_gap=false in both runs and is post-sweep selection logic; not the cause of 90->80 winner flip.
- pnl meta long_signals (327 -> 205) is sweep/PnL signal_count, not test-label positives.
- Test-label positives for winning long target are:
  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%
  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%
- Equal test trading days (212) is due to architecture:
  - one global date split is created from full features after removing 4 gap + 1 holdout day
  - split5 validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df
  - winners are evaluated/persisted on that shared window
- Reconstructed split5/test window for the later snapshot:
  - test: 2025-04-01 .. 2026-02-03 (212 dates)
  - gap: 2026-02-04 .. 2026-02-09
  - holdout: 2026-02-10
- Staged sweep folds are:
  - R1=1, R2=1, R3=2, R4=5, R5(extra groups)=5
  - no 3-fold final round in current sweep path
- Sweep-path training phase mostly reuses sweep-trained winners rather than retraining.

Additional reconstruction
- Replayed winning long execution path (vwap_rank) for projected entries:
  - run-20260210052705: proba>0.5 candidates=52, long_positions_taken=39
  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates=76, long_positions_taken=57
- Confirms distinction needed between:
  - long_signals (label opportunities)
  - long_positions (entered positions under execution constraints)

Hypotheses for divergence
- H1: One-day snapshot shift (2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10) changed fold boundaries/composition enough to flip close contests.
- H2: Config/algorithm drift (notably kelly in later run config/sweep) altered elimination dynamics and winner choice.
- H3: Reporting conflation (meta signal_count vs label positives) made changes appear impossible when comparing unlike metrics.

What remains
- Controlled A/B reruns:
  1) same snapshot, kelly off/on
  2) same algorithms, snapshot +1 day
  3) compare round histories/winner flips
- Reporting standardization across Slack/console/PDF/CSV:
  - long_signal_pct = positives / total_test_rows
  - long_signal_rate = positives / total_test_trading_days
  - keep sweep signal_count under separate label

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, post-exit-validity test)

New findings
- Re-tested the concern that PnL candidates were low because exits failed to compute.
- For both winning long runs, exit validity is 100% once a row is admitted as a candidate:
  - run-20260210052705: signals(proba>0.5)=52, baseline valid exits=52/52, stoploss=52/52, vwap=52/52
  - run-20260211053848: signals(proba>0.5)=76, baseline valid exits=76/76, stoploss=76/76, vwap=76/76
- Therefore, low candidate counts are NOT due to missing forward bars / missing entry / invalid exit rows in these runs.

Where the drop actually occurs
- Candidate gate for long classifier in sweep is hard-coded at predict_proba > 0.5.
- Label positives (TP+FN) are ~2.9k, but proba>0.5 rows are 52/76.
- This indicates a probability-thresholding/calibration mismatch rather than horizon-data completeness.

Sanity checks performed
- Not a class-direction inversion:
  - AUC(p) ~ 0.60/0.62
  - AUC(1-p) ~ 0.40/0.38
- So p is in the correct direction; values are just concentrated low.

Next step (in progress)
- Isolate raw base-model scores vs calibrated probabilities in sweep-trained winner artifacts to determine whether calibration compresses probabilities and suppresses candidate volume.

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, raw vs calibrated probabilities)

Raw-vs-calibrated analysis on saved winner models
- Loaded sweep winner artifacts and compared base model predict_proba vs calibrated model predict_proba on exact persisted test rows.
- Stored position_signals predict_proba matches calibrated output exactly (max abs diff ~1e-16).

Run-level results
- run-20260210052705 (long target from_2023):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 287
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 52
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6022)
  - calibration strongly reshapes probability scale and suppresses >0.5 candidates.
- run-20260211053848 (long target from_2021):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 90
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 76
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6232)
  - calibration effect on >0.5 count is smaller.

Implication
- Candidate collapse is primarily a probability-scale/calibration-threshold issue, not missing exit data.
- Current sweep candidate gate (proba > 0.5) is brittle across runs because calibration can change score scale while preserving ranking/AUC.

Likely bug class
- Metric/selection logic assumes a fixed semantic meaning for 0.5 after post-hoc calibration; this may be invalid for this problem and calibration procedure.

Linked under epic 1brutus-xu0d as active investigation/root-cause stream for implementation decisions.
### Handoff capsule (Kelly impact + signal/candidate findings)

What changed in this checkpoint
- Added a round-by-round comparison of yesterday vs today showing where Kelly changed algorithm rankings and elimination paths.
- Re-validated the long signal/candidate pipeline assumptions with concrete counts from persisted artifacts.

Key findings
- Shared test window behavior is confirmed (same test dates within a run across target start dates); differing start dates mainly change training history, not the sweep test fold.
- Long label-positive prevalence for winning targets is stable around ~19% of test rows (TP+FN from `global_metrics.json`), not ~3%.
- Low `pnl` candidate volume is not caused by missing exit computations:
  - Yesterday winner long: candidates 52 -> valid exits 52
  - Today winner long: candidates 76 -> valid exits 76
- Candidate collapse is consistent with fixed `predict_proba > 0.5` on calibrated probabilities:
  - Yesterday long winner: raw model >0.5 = 287, calibrated >0.5 = 52
  - Today long winner: raw model >0.5 = 90, calibrated >0.5 = 76
  - AUC raw vs calibrated unchanged in both runs (ranking preserved, scale shifted).

Kelly impact, round by round (examples)
- Long Round 1 (`from_2023` head-to-head):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d` with `vwap_rank`, contest_score `-0.551170` (rank 1).
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, contest_score `-0.012841` (rank 1).
- Long Round 2 (`from_2021` survivor):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d` with `stoploss`, score `-0.024692`.
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, score `-0.002590`.
- Long Round 3 (final long target duel, both runs using `vwap_rank`):
  - Yesterday winner: `...from_2023...` score `0.184970` vs `...from_2021...` score `-0.033495`.
  - Today winner: `...from_2021...` score `0.079508` vs `...from_2023...` score `0.027562`.
  - Interpretation: Kelly materially changed earlier-round algorithm competitiveness; final flip at R3 is also influenced by day+1 data drift.
- Short Round 1 illustrates broader reshuffling:
  - Yesterday top rows were mostly baseline/stoploss/vwap.
  - Today top contenders include Kelly variants (`kelly_0.5`, `kelly_0.25`) with different target survival order.

Hypotheses (most likely first)
1. Fixed post-calibration gate (`>0.5`) is too brittle; calibration scale drift can drastically alter tradable candidate volume without hurting rank quality.
2. Kelly introduction changed early-round contest scores and elimination paths, which changes which targets reach final rounds.
3. One extra market day plus short-threshold config change (90 -> 80) further perturbed rankings.

What remains
- Implement configurable long candidate gating policy (`1brutus-xu0d.1`) and wire metrics so we can compare candidate coverage under alternative gates.
- Run controlled A/B on yesterday/today artifacts (`1brutus-xu0d.2`) to isolate Kelly-vs-gate-vs-data effects.
- Add regression coverage for `long_signal_pct`, per-day signal rate, candidate counts, and projected position-open rate (`1brutus-xu0d.3`).

Verification steps
- Investigation-only checkpoint (no code edits in this update).
- For upcoming Python/model changes, verification target remains: `make model-sanity`.

What changed
- Implemented configurable long candidate gating in sweep PnL evaluation by replacing hard-coded `predict_proba > 0.5` with a resolved threshold policy in:
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/sweep_evaluator.py
- Added `long_prediction_threshold` wiring to sweep-time evaluator construction:
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/sweep_phase.py
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_executor.py
- Added focused regression coverage in:
  - model/tests/pipeline/test_pnl_roll_evaluator.py
  - verifies configured long threshold is honored
  - verifies non-long classifier path keeps legacy 0.5
  - verifies invalid threshold values fail fast

Behavioral outcome
- Sweep long classifier candidate generation is now policy-driven (`auto` adaptive valley or numeric threshold), not fixed at 0.5.
- Legacy fixed behavior remains available by setting `long_prediction_threshold = 0.5`.

What remains
- A/B evaluate yesterday vs today runs under explicit threshold settings (xu0d.2) to quantify candidate-volume and winner-path deltas.
- Complete reporting metric alignment workstream (6e7g): long_signal_pct vs long_signal_rate labels across Slack/console/PDF/CSV.
- Add broader regression coverage for signal/candidate/position metrics (xu0d.3).

HANDOFF CAPSULE (formatted)

Context
- Reopened this bead to continue yesterday-vs-today divergence analysis.

Established facts
- Compared runs:
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260210052705

  - short: target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d -> target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d
- short_normalize_gap=false in both runs and is post-sweep selection logic; not the cause of 90->80 winner flip.
- pnl meta long_signals (327 -> 205) is sweep/PnL signal_count, not test-label positives.
- Test-label positives for winning long target are:
  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%
  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%
- Equal test trading days (212) is due to architecture:
  - one global date split is created from full features after removing 4 gap + 1 holdout day
  - split5 validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df
  - winners are evaluated/persisted on that shared window

  - test: 2025-04-01 .. 2026-02-03 (212 dates)
  - gap: 2026-02-04 .. 2026-02-09
  - holdout: 2026-02-10

  - R1=1, R2=1, R3=2, R4=5, R5(extra groups)=5
  - no 3-fold final round in current sweep path

  - run-20260210052705: proba>0.5 candidates=52, long_positions_taken=39
  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates=76, long_positions_taken=57
- Confirms distinction needed between:
  - long_signals (label opportunities)
  - long_positions (entered positions under execution constraints)

Hypotheses for divergence
- H1: One-day snapshot shift (2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10) changed fold boundaries/composition enough to flip close contests.
- H2: Config/algorithm drift (notably kelly in later run config/sweep) altered elimination dynamics and winner choice.
- H3: Reporting conflation (meta signal_count vs label positives) made changes appear impossible when comparing unlike metrics.

What remains

  1) same snapshot, kelly off/on
  2) same algorithms, snapshot +1 day

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, post-exit-validity test)

New findings
- Re-tested the concern that PnL candidates were low because exits failed to compute.
- For both winning long runs, exit validity is 100% once a row is admitted as a candidate:
  - run-20260210052705: signals(proba>0.5)=52, baseline valid exits=52/52, stoploss=52/52, vwap=52/52
  - run-20260211053848: signals(proba>0.5)=76, baseline valid exits=76/76, stoploss=76/76, vwap=76/76
- Therefore, low candidate counts are NOT due to missing forward bars / missing entry / invalid exit rows in these runs.

Where the drop actually occurs
- Candidate gate for long classifier in sweep is hard-coded at predict_proba > 0.5.
- Label positives (TP+FN) are ~2.9k, but proba>0.5 rows are 52/76.
- This indicates a probability-thresholding/calibration mismatch rather than horizon-data completeness.

Sanity checks performed
- Not a class-direction inversion:
  - AUC(p) ~ 0.60/0.62
  - AUC(1-p) ~ 0.40/0.38
- So p is in the correct direction; values are just concentrated low.

Next step (in progress)
- Isolate raw base-model scores vs calibrated probabilities in sweep-trained winner artifacts to determine whether calibration compresses probabilities and suppresses candidate volume.

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, raw vs calibrated probabilities)

Raw-vs-calibrated analysis on saved winner models
- Loaded sweep winner artifacts and compared base model predict_proba vs calibrated model predict_proba on exact persisted test rows.
- Stored position_signals predict_proba matches calibrated output exactly (max abs diff ~1e-16).

Run-level results
- run-20260210052705 (long target from_2023):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 287
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 52
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6022)
  - calibration strongly reshapes probability scale and suppresses >0.5 candidates.
- run-20260211053848 (long target from_2021):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 90
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 76
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6232)
  - calibration effect on >0.5 count is smaller.

Implication
- Candidate collapse is primarily a probability-scale/calibration-threshold issue, not missing exit data.
- Current sweep candidate gate (proba > 0.5) is brittle across runs because calibration can change score scale while preserving ranking/AUC.

Likely bug class
- Metric/selection logic assumes a fixed semantic meaning for 0.5 after post-hoc calibration; this may be invalid for this problem and calibration procedure.

Linked under epic 1brutus-xu0d as active investigation/root-cause stream for implementation decisions.
### Handoff capsule (Kelly impact + signal/candidate findings)

What changed in this checkpoint
- Added a round-by-round comparison of yesterday vs today showing where Kelly changed algorithm rankings and elimination paths.
- Re-validated the long signal/candidate pipeline assumptions with concrete counts from persisted artifacts.

Key findings
- Shared test window behavior is confirmed (same test dates within a run across target start dates); differing start dates mainly change training history, not the sweep test fold.
- Long label-positive prevalence for winning targets is stable around ~19% of test rows (TP+FN from `global_metrics.json`), not ~3%.
- Low `pnl` candidate volume is not caused by missing exit computations:
  - Yesterday winner long: candidates 52 -> valid exits 52
  - Today winner long: candidates 76 -> valid exits 76
- Candidate collapse is consistent with fixed `predict_proba > 0.5` on calibrated probabilities:
  - Yesterday long winner: raw model >0.5 = 287, calibrated >0.5 = 52
  - Today long winner: raw model >0.5 = 90, calibrated >0.5 = 76
  - AUC raw vs calibrated unchanged in both runs (ranking preserved, scale shifted).

Kelly impact, round by round (examples)
- Long Round 1 (`from_2023` head-to-head):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d` with `vwap_rank`, contest_score `-0.551170` (rank 1).
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, contest_score `-0.012841` (rank 1).
- Long Round 2 (`from_2021` survivor):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d` with `stoploss`, score `-0.024692`.
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, score `-0.002590`.
- Long Round 3 (final long target duel, both runs using `vwap_rank`):
  - Yesterday winner: `...from_2023...` score `0.184970` vs `...from_2021...` score `-0.033495`.
  - Today winner: `...from_2021...` score `0.079508` vs `...from_2023...` score `0.027562`.
  - Interpretation: Kelly materially changed earlier-round algorithm competitiveness; final flip at R3 is also influenced by day+1 data drift.
- Short Round 1 illustrates broader reshuffling:
  - Yesterday top rows were mostly baseline/stoploss/vwap.
  - Today top contenders include Kelly variants (`kelly_0.5`, `kelly_0.25`) with different target survival order.

Hypotheses (most likely first)
1. Fixed post-calibration gate (`>0.5`) is too brittle; calibration scale drift can drastically alter tradable candidate volume without hurting rank quality.
2. Kelly introduction changed early-round contest scores and elimination paths, which changes which targets reach final rounds.
3. One extra market day plus short-threshold config change (90 -> 80) further perturbed rankings.

What remains
- Implement configurable long candidate gating policy (`1brutus-xu0d.1`) and wire metrics so we can compare candidate coverage under alternative gates.
- Run controlled A/B on yesterday/today artifacts (`1brutus-xu0d.2`) to isolate Kelly-vs-gate-vs-data effects.
- Add regression coverage for `long_signal_pct`, per-day signal rate, candidate counts, and projected position-open rate (`1brutus-xu0d.3`).

  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/sweep_evaluator.py

  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_executor.py

  - verifies configured long threshold is honored
  - verifies non-long classifier path keeps legacy 0.5
  - verifies invalid threshold values fail fast

Behavioral outcome
- Sweep long classifier candidate generation is now policy-driven (`auto` adaptive valley or numeric threshold), not fixed at 0.5.
- Legacy fixed behavior remains available by setting `long_prediction_threshold = 0.5`.

What remains

HANDOFF CAPSULE (formatted)

Context
- Reopened this bead to continue yesterday-vs-today divergence analysis.
- Side detour: 1brutus-6e7g tracks reporting cleanup for long signal pct/rate naming.

Established facts
- Compared runs:
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260210052705

  - short: target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d -> target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d
- short_normalize_gap=false in both runs and is post-sweep selection logic; not the cause of 90->80 winner flip.
- pnl meta long_signals (327 -> 205) is sweep/PnL signal_count, not test-label positives.
- Test-label positives for winning long target are:
  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%
  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%
- Equal test trading days (212) is due to architecture:
  - one global date split is created from full features after removing 4 gap + 1 holdout day
  - split5 validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df
  - winners are evaluated/persisted on that shared window

  - run-20260210052705: proba>0.5 candidates=52, long_positions_taken=39
  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates=76, long_positions_taken=57
- Confirms distinction needed between:
  - long_signals (label opportunities)
  - long_positions (entered positions under execution constraints)

Hypotheses for divergence
- H1: One-day snapshot shift (2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10) changed fold boundaries/composition enough to flip close contests.
- H2: Config/algorithm drift (notably kelly in later run config/sweep) altered elimination dynamics and winner choice.
- H3: Reporting conflation (meta signal_count vs label positives) made changes appear impossible when comparing unlike metrics.

What remains

  1) same snapshot, kelly off/on
  2) same algorithms, snapshot +1 day

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, post-exit-validity test)

New findings
- Re-tested the concern that PnL candidates were low because exits failed to compute.
- For both winning long runs, exit validity is 100% once a row is admitted as a candidate:
  - run-20260210052705: signals(proba>0.5)=52, baseline valid exits=52/52, stoploss=52/52, vwap=52/52
  - run-20260211053848: signals(proba>0.5)=76, baseline valid exits=76/76, stoploss=76/76, vwap=76/76
- Therefore, low candidate counts are NOT due to missing forward bars / missing entry / invalid exit rows in these runs.

Where the drop actually occurs
- Candidate gate for long classifier in sweep is hard-coded at predict_proba > 0.5.
- Label positives (TP+FN) are ~2.9k, but proba>0.5 rows are 52/76.
- This indicates a probability-thresholding/calibration mismatch rather than horizon-data completeness.

Sanity checks performed
- Not a class-direction inversion:
  - AUC(p) ~ 0.60/0.62
  - AUC(1-p) ~ 0.40/0.38
- So p is in the correct direction; values are just concentrated low.

Next step (in progress)
- Isolate raw base-model scores vs calibrated probabilities in sweep-trained winner artifacts to determine whether calibration compresses probabilities and suppresses candidate volume.

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, raw vs calibrated probabilities)

Raw-vs-calibrated analysis on saved winner models
- Loaded sweep winner artifacts and compared base model predict_proba vs calibrated model predict_proba on exact persisted test rows.
- Stored position_signals predict_proba matches calibrated output exactly (max abs diff ~1e-16).

Run-level results
- run-20260210052705 (long target from_2023):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 287
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 52
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6022)
  - calibration strongly reshapes probability scale and suppresses >0.5 candidates.
- run-20260211053848 (long target from_2021):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 90
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 76
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6232)
  - calibration effect on >0.5 count is smaller.

Implication
- Candidate collapse is primarily a probability-scale/calibration-threshold issue, not missing exit data.
- Current sweep candidate gate (proba > 0.5) is brittle across runs because calibration can change score scale while preserving ranking/AUC.

Likely bug class
- Metric/selection logic assumes a fixed semantic meaning for 0.5 after post-hoc calibration; this may be invalid for this problem and calibration procedure.

Linked under epic 1brutus-xu0d as active investigation/root-cause stream for implementation decisions.
### Handoff capsule (Kelly impact + signal/candidate findings)

What changed in this checkpoint
- Added a round-by-round comparison of yesterday vs today showing where Kelly changed algorithm rankings and elimination paths.
- Re-validated the long signal/candidate pipeline assumptions with concrete counts from persisted artifacts.

Key findings
- Shared test window behavior is confirmed (same test dates within a run across target start dates); differing start dates mainly change training history, not the sweep test fold.
- Long label-positive prevalence for winning targets is stable around ~19% of test rows (TP+FN from `global_metrics.json`), not ~3%.
- Low `pnl` candidate volume is not caused by missing exit computations:
  - Yesterday winner long: candidates 52 -> valid exits 52
  - Today winner long: candidates 76 -> valid exits 76
- Candidate collapse is consistent with fixed `predict_proba > 0.5` on calibrated probabilities:
  - Yesterday long winner: raw model >0.5 = 287, calibrated >0.5 = 52
  - Today long winner: raw model >0.5 = 90, calibrated >0.5 = 76
  - AUC raw vs calibrated unchanged in both runs (ranking preserved, scale shifted).

Kelly impact, round by round (examples)
- Long Round 1 (`from_2023` head-to-head):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d` with `vwap_rank`, contest_score `-0.551170` (rank 1).
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, contest_score `-0.012841` (rank 1).
- Long Round 2 (`from_2021` survivor):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d` with `stoploss`, score `-0.024692`.
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, score `-0.002590`.
- Long Round 3 (final long target duel, both runs using `vwap_rank`):
  - Yesterday winner: `...from_2023...` score `0.184970` vs `...from_2021...` score `-0.033495`.
  - Today winner: `...from_2021...` score `0.079508` vs `...from_2023...` score `0.027562`.
  - Interpretation: Kelly materially changed earlier-round algorithm competitiveness; final flip at R3 is also influenced by day+1 data drift.
- Short Round 1 illustrates broader reshuffling:
  - Yesterday top rows were mostly baseline/stoploss/vwap.
  - Today top contenders include Kelly variants (`kelly_0.5`, `kelly_0.25`) with different target survival order.

Hypotheses (most likely first)
1. Fixed post-calibration gate (`>0.5`) is too brittle; calibration scale drift can drastically alter tradable candidate volume without hurting rank quality.
2. Kelly introduction changed early-round contest scores and elimination paths, which changes which targets reach final rounds.
3. One extra market day plus short-threshold config change (90 -> 80) further perturbed rankings.

What remains
- Implement configurable long candidate gating policy (`1brutus-xu0d.1`) and wire metrics so we can compare candidate coverage under alternative gates.
- Run controlled A/B on yesterday/today artifacts (`1brutus-xu0d.2`) to isolate Kelly-vs-gate-vs-data effects.
- Add regression coverage for `long_signal_pct`, per-day signal rate, candidate counts, and projected position-open rate (`1brutus-xu0d.3`).

Behavioral outcome

HANDOFF CAPSULE (formatted)

Context
- Reopened this bead to continue yesterday-vs-today divergence analysis.
- Side detour: 1brutus-6e7g tracks reporting cleanup for long signal pct/rate naming.

Established facts
- Compared runs:
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260210052705
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260211053848

  - short: target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d -> target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d
- short_normalize_gap=false in both runs and is post-sweep selection logic; not the cause of 90->80 winner flip.
- pnl meta long_signals (327 -> 205) is sweep/PnL signal_count, not test-label positives.
- Test-label positives for winning long target are:
  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%
  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%
- Equal test trading days (212) is due to architecture:
  - one global date split is created from full features after removing 4 gap + 1 holdout day
  - split5 validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df
  - winners are evaluated/persisted on that shared window
- Reconstructed split5/test window for the later snapshot:
  - test: 2025-04-01 .. 2026-02-03 (212 dates)
  - gap: 2026-02-04 .. 2026-02-09
  - holdout: 2026-02-10
- Staged sweep folds are:
  - R1=1, R2=1, R3=2, R4=5, R5(extra groups)=5
  - no 3-fold final round in current sweep path

Additional reconstruction

  - run-20260210052705: proba>0.5 candidates=52, long_positions_taken=39
  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates=76, long_positions_taken=57
- Confirms distinction needed between:
  - long_signals (label opportunities)
  - long_positions (entered positions under execution constraints)

Hypotheses for divergence
- H1: One-day snapshot shift (2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10) changed fold boundaries/composition enough to flip close contests.
- H2: Config/algorithm drift (notably kelly in later run config/sweep) altered elimination dynamics and winner choice.
- H3: Reporting conflation (meta signal_count vs label positives) made changes appear impossible when comparing unlike metrics.

What remains
- Controlled A/B reruns:
  1) same snapshot, kelly off/on
  2) same algorithms, snapshot +1 day
  3) compare round histories/winner flips
- Reporting standardization across Slack/console/PDF/CSV:

  - long_signal_rate = positives / total_test_trading_days

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, post-exit-validity test)

New findings
- Re-tested the concern that PnL candidates were low because exits failed to compute.
- For both winning long runs, exit validity is 100% once a row is admitted as a candidate:
  - run-20260210052705: signals(proba>0.5)=52, baseline valid exits=52/52, stoploss=52/52, vwap=52/52
  - run-20260211053848: signals(proba>0.5)=76, baseline valid exits=76/76, stoploss=76/76, vwap=76/76
- Therefore, low candidate counts are NOT due to missing forward bars / missing entry / invalid exit rows in these runs.

Where the drop actually occurs
- Candidate gate for long classifier in sweep is hard-coded at predict_proba > 0.5.
- Label positives (TP+FN) are ~2.9k, but proba>0.5 rows are 52/76.
- This indicates a probability-thresholding/calibration mismatch rather than horizon-data completeness.

Sanity checks performed
- Not a class-direction inversion:
  - AUC(p) ~ 0.60/0.62
  - AUC(1-p) ~ 0.40/0.38
- So p is in the correct direction; values are just concentrated low.

Next step (in progress)
- Isolate raw base-model scores vs calibrated probabilities in sweep-trained winner artifacts to determine whether calibration compresses probabilities and suppresses candidate volume.

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, raw vs calibrated probabilities)

Raw-vs-calibrated analysis on saved winner models
- Loaded sweep winner artifacts and compared base model predict_proba vs calibrated model predict_proba on exact persisted test rows.
- Stored position_signals predict_proba matches calibrated output exactly (max abs diff ~1e-16).

Run-level results
- run-20260210052705 (long target from_2023):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 287
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 52
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6022)
  - calibration strongly reshapes probability scale and suppresses >0.5 candidates.
- run-20260211053848 (long target from_2021):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 90
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 76
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6232)
  - calibration effect on >0.5 count is smaller.

Implication
- Candidate collapse is primarily a probability-scale/calibration-threshold issue, not missing exit data.
- Current sweep candidate gate (proba > 0.5) is brittle across runs because calibration can change score scale while preserving ranking/AUC.

Likely bug class
- Metric/selection logic assumes a fixed semantic meaning for 0.5 after post-hoc calibration; this may be invalid for this problem and calibration procedure.

Linked under epic 1brutus-xu0d as active investigation/root-cause stream for implementation decisions.
### Handoff capsule (Kelly impact + signal/candidate findings)

What changed in this checkpoint
- Added a round-by-round comparison of yesterday vs today showing where Kelly changed algorithm rankings and elimination paths.
- Re-validated the long signal/candidate pipeline assumptions with concrete counts from persisted artifacts.

Key findings
- Shared test window behavior is confirmed (same test dates within a run across target start dates); differing start dates mainly change training history, not the sweep test fold.
- Long label-positive prevalence for winning targets is stable around ~19% of test rows (TP+FN from `global_metrics.json`), not ~3%.
- Low `pnl` candidate volume is not caused by missing exit computations:
  - Yesterday winner long: candidates 52 -> valid exits 52
  - Today winner long: candidates 76 -> valid exits 76
- Candidate collapse is consistent with fixed `predict_proba > 0.5` on calibrated probabilities:
  - Yesterday long winner: raw model >0.5 = 287, calibrated >0.5 = 52
  - Today long winner: raw model >0.5 = 90, calibrated >0.5 = 76
  - AUC raw vs calibrated unchanged in both runs (ranking preserved, scale shifted).

Kelly impact, round by round (examples)
- Long Round 1 (`from_2023` head-to-head):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d` with `vwap_rank`, contest_score `-0.551170` (rank 1).
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, contest_score `-0.012841` (rank 1).
- Long Round 2 (`from_2021` survivor):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d` with `stoploss`, score `-0.024692`.
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, score `-0.002590`.
- Long Round 3 (final long target duel, both runs using `vwap_rank`):
  - Yesterday winner: `...from_2023...` score `0.184970` vs `...from_2021...` score `-0.033495`.
  - Today winner: `...from_2021...` score `0.079508` vs `...from_2023...` score `0.027562`.
  - Interpretation: Kelly materially changed earlier-round algorithm competitiveness; final flip at R3 is also influenced by day+1 data drift.
- Short Round 1 illustrates broader reshuffling:
  - Yesterday top rows were mostly baseline/stoploss/vwap.
  - Today top contenders include Kelly variants (`kelly_0.5`, `kelly_0.25`) with different target survival order.

Hypotheses (most likely first)
1. Fixed post-calibration gate (`>0.5`) is too brittle; calibration scale drift can drastically alter tradable candidate volume without hurting rank quality.
2. Kelly introduction changed early-round contest scores and elimination paths, which changes which targets reach final rounds.
3. One extra market day plus short-threshold config change (90 -> 80) further perturbed rankings.

What remains
- Implement configurable long candidate gating policy (`1brutus-xu0d.1`) and wire metrics so we can compare candidate coverage under alternative gates.
- Run controlled A/B on yesterday/today artifacts (`1brutus-xu0d.2`) to isolate Kelly-vs-gate-vs-data effects.
- Add regression coverage for `long_signal_pct`, per-day signal rate, candidate counts, and projected position-open rate (`1brutus-xu0d.3`).

  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/sweep_evaluator.py
- Added `long_prediction_threshold` wiring to sweep-time evaluator construction:
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/sweep_phase.py
  - model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_executor.py

  - verifies configured long threshold is honored
  - verifies non-long classifier path keeps legacy 0.5
  - verifies invalid threshold values fail fast

Behavioral outcome
- Sweep long classifier candidate generation is now policy-driven (`auto` adaptive valley or numeric threshold), not fixed at 0.5.
- Legacy fixed behavior remains available by setting `long_prediction_threshold = 0.5`.

What remains

- Complete reporting metric alignment workstream (6e7g): long_signal_pct vs long_signal_rate labels across Slack/console/PDF/CSV.

HANDOFF CAPSULE (formatted)

Context

- Test-label positives for winning long target are:
  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%
  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%

  - split5 validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df
  - winners are evaluated/persisted on that shared window

  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates=76, long_positions_taken=57
- Confirms distinction needed between:
  - long_signals (label opportunities)
  - long_positions (entered positions under execution constraints)

Hypotheses for divergence
- H1: One-day snapshot shift (2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10) changed fold boundaries/composition enough to flip close contests.
- H2: Config/algorithm drift (notably kelly in later run config/sweep) altered elimination dynamics and winner choice.
- H3: Reporting conflation (meta signal_count vs label positives) made changes appear impossible when comparing unlike metrics.

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, post-exit-validity test)

New findings
- Re-tested the concern that PnL candidates were low because exits failed to compute.
- For both winning long runs, exit validity is 100% once a row is admitted as a candidate:
  - run-20260210052705: signals(proba>0.5)=52, baseline valid exits=52/52, stoploss=52/52, vwap=52/52
  - run-20260211053848: signals(proba>0.5)=76, baseline valid exits=76/76, stoploss=76/76, vwap=76/76
- Therefore, low candidate counts are NOT due to missing forward bars / missing entry / invalid exit rows in these runs.

Where the drop actually occurs
- Candidate gate for long classifier in sweep is hard-coded at predict_proba > 0.5.
- Label positives (TP+FN) are ~2.9k, but proba>0.5 rows are 52/76.
- This indicates a probability-thresholding/calibration mismatch rather than horizon-data completeness.

Sanity checks performed
- Not a class-direction inversion:
  - AUC(p) ~ 0.60/0.62
  - AUC(1-p) ~ 0.40/0.38
- So p is in the correct direction; values are just concentrated low.

Next step (in progress)
- Isolate raw base-model scores vs calibrated probabilities in sweep-trained winner artifacts to determine whether calibration compresses probabilities and suppresses candidate volume.

CHECKPOINT UPDATE (2026-02-11, raw vs calibrated probabilities)

Raw-vs-calibrated analysis on saved winner models
- Loaded sweep winner artifacts and compared base model predict_proba vs calibrated model predict_proba on exact persisted test rows.
- Stored position_signals predict_proba matches calibrated output exactly (max abs diff ~1e-16).

Run-level results
- run-20260210052705 (long target from_2023):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 287
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 52
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6022)
  - calibration strongly reshapes probability scale and suppresses >0.5 candidates.
- run-20260211053848 (long target from_2021):
  - count(raw > 0.5) = 90
  - count(calibrated > 0.5) = 76
  - AUC unchanged (raw=cal=0.6232)
  - calibration effect on >0.5 count is smaller.

Implication
- Candidate collapse is primarily a probability-scale/calibration-threshold issue, not missing exit data.
- Current sweep candidate gate (proba > 0.5) is brittle across runs because calibration can change score scale while preserving ranking/AUC.

Likely bug class
- Metric/selection logic assumes a fixed semantic meaning for 0.5 after post-hoc calibration; this may be invalid for this problem and calibration procedure.

Linked under epic 1brutus-xu0d as active investigation/root-cause stream for implementation decisions.
### Handoff capsule (Kelly impact + signal/candidate findings)

What changed in this checkpoint
- Added a round-by-round comparison of yesterday vs today showing where Kelly changed algorithm rankings and elimination paths.
- Re-validated the long signal/candidate pipeline assumptions with concrete counts from persisted artifacts.

Key findings
- Shared test window behavior is confirmed (same test dates within a run across target start dates); differing start dates mainly change training history, not the sweep test fold.
- Long label-positive prevalence for winning targets is stable around ~19% of test rows (TP+FN from `global_metrics.json`), not ~3%.
- Low `pnl` candidate volume is not caused by missing exit computations:
  - Yesterday winner long: candidates 52 -> valid exits 52
  - Today winner long: candidates 76 -> valid exits 76
- Candidate collapse is consistent with fixed `predict_proba > 0.5` on calibrated probabilities:
  - Yesterday long winner: raw model >0.5 = 287, calibrated >0.5 = 52
  - Today long winner: raw model >0.5 = 90, calibrated >0.5 = 76
  - AUC raw vs calibrated unchanged in both runs (ranking preserved, scale shifted).

Kelly impact, round by round (examples)
- Long Round 1 (`from_2023` head-to-head):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2023_class_5d` with `vwap_rank`, contest_score `-0.551170` (rank 1).
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, contest_score `-0.012841` (rank 1).
- Long Round 2 (`from_2021` survivor):
  - Yesterday: `target_long_abs_3p5_std_0p7_from_2021_class_5d` with `stoploss`, score `-0.024692`.
  - Today: same target with `kelly_0.25`, score `-0.002590`.
- Long Round 3 (final long target duel, both runs using `vwap_rank`):
  - Yesterday winner: `...from_2023...` score `0.184970` vs `...from_2021...` score `-0.033495`.
  - Today winner: `...from_2021...` score `0.079508` vs `...from_2023...` score `0.027562`.
  - Interpretation: Kelly materially changed earlier-round algorithm competitiveness; final flip at R3 is also influenced by day+1 data drift.
- Short Round 1 illustrates broader reshuffling:
  - Yesterday top rows were mostly baseline/stoploss/vwap.
  - Today top contenders include Kelly variants (`kelly_0.5`, `kelly_0.25`) with different target survival order.

Hypotheses (most likely first)
1. Fixed post-calibration gate (`>0.5`) is too brittle; calibration scale drift can drastically alter tradable candidate volume without hurting rank quality.
2. Kelly introduction changed early-round contest scores and elimination paths, which changes which targets reach final rounds.
3. One extra market day plus short-threshold config change (90 -> 80) further perturbed rankings.

What remains
- Implement configurable long candidate gating policy (`1brutus-xu0d.1`) and wire metrics so we can compare candidate coverage under alternative gates.
- Run controlled A/B on yesterday/today artifacts (`1brutus-xu0d.2`) to isolate Kelly-vs-gate-vs-data effects.
- Add regression coverage for `long_signal_pct`, per-day signal rate, candidate counts, and projected position-open rate (`1brutus-xu0d.3`).

HANDOFF CAPSULE (formatted)

Context
- Reopened this bead to continue yesterday-vs-today divergence analysis.

Established facts
- Compared runs:
  - /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/model/data/results/run-20260210052705

  - short: target_short_pct_90_win_63_from_2023_class_2d -> target_short_pct_80_win_63_from_2023_class_2d
- short_normalize_gap=false in both runs and is post-sweep selection logic; not the cause of 90->80 winner flip.
- pnl meta long_signals (327 -> 205) is sweep/PnL signal_count, not test-label positives.
- Test-label positives for winning long target are:
  - run-20260210052705: 2889 / 15114 = 19.1%
  - run-20260211053848: 2916 / 15115 = 19.3%
- Equal test trading days (212) is due to architecture:
  - one global date split is created from full features after removing 4 gap + 1 holdout day
  - split5 validation window becomes shared sweep_test_df
  - winners are evaluated/persisted on that shared window

  - gap: 2026-02-04 .. 2026-02-09

  - run-20260210052705: proba>0.5 candidates=52, long_positions_taken=39
  - run-20260211053848: proba>0.5 candidates=76, long_positions_taken=57
- Confirms distinction needed between:
  - long_signals (label opportunities)
  - long_positions (entered positions under execution constraints)

Hypotheses for divergence
- H1: One-day snapshot shift (2026-02-09 -> 2026-02-10) changed fold boundaries/composition enough to flip close contests.
- H2: Config/algorithm drift (notably kelly in later run config/sweep) altered elimination dynamics and winner choice.
- H3: Reporting conflation (meta signal_count vs label positives) made changes appear impossible when comparing unlike metrics.

What remains

  1) same snapshot, kelly off/on
