{"id":"brutus-l1lf.4","title":"Fork contest training off Monday baseline; remove unswept/fresh-train fallback from prod path","description":"Create an isolated contest training path cloned from Monday (2026-02-16) behavior, keep production train_unified path unchanged, and enforce no unswept/fresh-train fallback in contest flow.","notes":"Handoff capsule (2026-02-20): investigate all-instance contest failure run 20260220183304-2e4b

What happened
- All 4 shard tasks (`long-shard-001`, `long-shard-002`, `short-shard-001`, `short-shard-002`) failed on attempt 1 with orchestration exit_code=201 and failure_kind=task_error in `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/orchestration_state.json`.
- Each shard completed side-specific sweep and selected exactly one target (`has_long=True has_short=False` for long shards, inverse for short shards), then failed at persistence merge.

Root cause
- `_train_all_targets` creates sweep-reused `TrainingResult` objects with `training_df=pd.DataFrame()` (empty, no `ticker`/`trade_date`) at `model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/training_phase.py:194-206`.
- `_merge_training_dataframes` now requires at least one `training_df` containing merge keys and raises if none exist at `model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/persistence_phase.py:50-59`.
- In side-isolated shards, all trained targets are sweep-reused for that one side, so no fresh-trained `training_df` exists to satisfy merge-key requirement, causing deterministic failure.

Evidence
- Long shard failure stacktrace: `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/shards/long-shard-001/stderr.log:118-130`.
- Short shard failure stacktrace: `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/shards/short-shard-002/stderr.log:116-129`.
- Orchestration task metadata (exit_code 201, task_error): `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/orchestration_state.json:705-737`.
- Side-isolation override is present (`settings.selected_short = {}` on long shard) and functioning as intended: `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/orchestration_state.json:698-702`.

Follow-up tracking
- Created bug bead `ws08`: "Contest shard crash when sweep-reused targets have empty training_df".

What remains
- Patch sweep-reuse path so persistence merge has a valid base frame (e.g., preserve key columns in reused `training_df`, or teach persistence merge to derive merge base from test/holdout context/offline_features when training_df lacks keys).
- Add regression test for side-isolated one-target shard flow (long-only and short-only) that currently reproduces this failure.
- Re-run contest orchestration after fix and verify all shard tasks reach persistence without merge-key errors.

What happened
- All 4 shard tasks (`long-shard-001`, `long-shard-002`, `short-shard-001`, `short-shard-002`) failed on attempt 1 with orchestration exit_code=201 and failure_kind=task_error in `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/orchestration_state.json`.
- Each shard completed side-specific sweep and selected exactly one target (`has_long=True has_short=False` for long shards, inverse for short shards), then failed at persistence merge.

Root cause
- `_train_all_targets` creates sweep-reused `TrainingResult` objects with `training_df=pd.DataFrame()` (empty, no `ticker`/`trade_date`) at `model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/training_phase.py:194-206`.

- In side-isolated shards, all trained targets are sweep-reused for that one side, so no fresh-trained `training_df` exists to satisfy merge-key requirement, causing deterministic failure.

Evidence
- Long shard failure stacktrace: `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/shards/long-shard-001/stderr.log:118-130`.
- Short shard failure stacktrace: `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/shards/short-shard-002/stderr.log:116-129`.

Root cause analysis (2026-02-23):
- Crash occurs on single-node side-winner path: _select_global_winner -> _try_contest_winner -> select_contest_winner.
- round_robin_selection.py used "value or 0.0"; NaN is truthy in Python, so NaN propagated into tie-break metrics.
- select_contest_winner tie filters used abs(a-b) <= epsilon; with NaN/Inf this can drop all candidates and trigger IndexError on tied[0].

Fixes implemented:
- Hardened contest tie equality for NaN/Inf in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/contest.py.
- Sanitized NaN contest inputs in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_selection.py (_coerce_nan + score fallback).
- Added regressions in model/tests/pipeline/test_contest.py and model/tests/pipeline/test_round_robin.py.

{"id":"brutus-l1lf.4","title":"Fork contest training off Monday baseline; remove unswept/fresh-train fallback from prod path","description":"Create an isolated contest training path cloned from Monday (2026-02-16) behavior, keep production train_unified path unchanged, and enforce no unswept/fresh-train fallback in contest flow.","notes":"Handoff capsule (2026-02-20): investigate all-instance contest failure run 20260220183304-2e4b

What happened
- All 4 shard tasks (`long-shard-001`, `long-shard-002`, `short-shard-001`, `short-shard-002`) failed on attempt 1 with orchestration exit_code=201 and failure_kind=task_error in `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/orchestration_state.json`.
- Each shard completed side-specific sweep and selected exactly one target (`has_long=True has_short=False` for long shards, inverse for short shards), then failed at persistence merge.

Root cause
- `_train_all_targets` creates sweep-reused `TrainingResult` objects with `training_df=pd.DataFrame()` (empty, no `ticker`/`trade_date`) at `model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/training_phase.py:194-206`.
- `_merge_training_dataframes` now requires at least one `training_df` containing merge keys and raises if none exist at `model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/persistence_phase.py:50-59`.
- In side-isolated shards, all trained targets are sweep-reused for that one side, so no fresh-trained `training_df` exists to satisfy merge-key requirement, causing deterministic failure.

Evidence
- Long shard failure stacktrace: `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/shards/long-shard-001/stderr.log:118-130`.
- Short shard failure stacktrace: `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/shards/short-shard-002/stderr.log:116-129`.
- Orchestration task metadata (exit_code 201, task_error): `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/orchestration_state.json:705-737`.
- Side-isolation override is present (`settings.selected_short = {}` on long shard) and functioning as intended: `model/data/results/orchestration/20260220183304-2e4b/orchestration_state.json:698-702`.

Follow-up tracking
- Created bug bead `ws08`: "Contest shard crash when sweep-reused targets have empty training_df".

What remains
- Patch sweep-reuse path so persistence merge has a valid base frame (e.g., preserve key columns in reused `training_df`, or teach persistence merge to derive merge base from test/holdout context/offline_features when training_df lacks keys).
- Add regression test for side-isolated one-target shard flow (long-only and short-only) that currently reproduces this failure.
- Re-run contest orchestration after fix and verify all shard tasks reach persistence without merge-key errors.

Root cause analysis (2026-02-23):
- Crash occurs on single-node side-winner path: _select_global_winner -> _try_contest_winner -> select_contest_winner.
- round_robin_selection.py used "value or 0.0"; NaN is truthy in Python, so NaN propagated into tie-break metrics.
- select_contest_winner tie filters used abs(a-b) <= epsilon; with NaN/Inf this can drop all candidates and trigger IndexError on tied[0].

Fixes implemented:
- Hardened contest tie equality for NaN/Inf in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/contest.py.
- Sanitized NaN contest inputs in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_selection.py (_coerce_nan + score fallback).
- Added regressions in model/tests/pipeline/test_contest.py and model/tests/pipeline/test_round_robin.py.

Investigation update (2026-02-23): build_features_orchestration failure is reproducible from manifest configuration mismatch, not from missing code in pipeline/build_features or pipeline/train_unified. Current orchestration manifest axes set long horizon_length=[2,3] (model/data/orchestration_manifest.toml), while settings.selected_long.target remains *_class_5d. Feature generation therefore only materializes 2d/3d horizon columns, but generic target embedding still requests 5d source columns (offline_*_class_5d), triggering KeyError. Historical archived manifest model/data/orchestration_manifest_20260220_091256.toml had axes long horizon_length=[2,3,4,5], which explains prior successful runs under same code path.

Root cause analysis (2026-02-23):
- Crash occurs on single-node side-winner path: _select_global_winner -> _try_contest_winner -> select_contest_winner.
- round_robin_selection.py used "value or 0.0"; NaN is truthy in Python, so NaN propagated into tie-break metrics.
- select_contest_winner tie filters used abs(a-b) <= epsilon; with NaN/Inf this can drop all candidates and trigger IndexError on tied[0].

Fixes implemented:
- Hardened contest tie equality for NaN/Inf in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/pnl_roll/contest.py.
- Sanitized NaN contest inputs in model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/round_robin_selection.py (_coerce_nan + score fallback).
- Added regressions in model/tests/pipeline/test_contest.py and model/tests/pipeline/test_round_robin.py.

{"id":"brutus-75fh","title":"Hard-separate single-node and multi-node AWS training lanes","notes":"HANDOFF CAPSULE (2026-02-24)

What changed
- Implemented hard lane separation across single-node and multi-node AWS training paths.
- S3 namespace isolation for contest lane:
  - Contest features default to s3://brutus-data-<env>/features/orchestration/...
  - Contest manifests default to s3://brutus-data-<env>/orchestration/contests/manifests/...
  - Contest path now rejects s3://brutus-data-<env>/features/unified/... inputs.
- EC2 pool isolation:
  - Added BrutusLane=single|contest tagging and lane-based instance discovery/selection.
  - Single-node orchestration defaults to --instance-lane single.
  - Contest shard wiring enforces --instance-lane contest.
  - Contest cleanup is lane-scoped (with legacy fallback to BrutusContest=true for older nodes).
- Venv artifact S3 isolation (lane partitioning):
  - Single: s3://brutus-data-<env>/venv/single/latest-<arch>.txt
  - Contest: s3://brutus-data-<env>/venv/contest/latest-<arch>.txt
  - upload_venv.sh and task aws:model:upload-venv now support lane-scoped pointer updates via --instance-lane.
- Task/docs updated to reflect separation contract and lane-scoped cleanup/usage.
- Added/updated regression tests for:
  - lane-filtered EC2 discovery and launch wiring,
  - contest command contract forwarding of --instance-lane,
  - contest cleanup selector behavior,
  - contest S3 namespace guardrails,
  - lane-scoped venv pointer discovery.

Files / key edits
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/scripts/aws/contest_orchestration/manifest.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/scripts/aws/contest_orchestration/runtime.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/scripts/aws/deploy_to_ec2.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/scripts/aws/orchestrate.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/scripts/aws/orchestrate_remote_contest.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/scripts/aws/upload_venv.sh
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/tasks/aws.yml
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/docs/distributed-contests.md
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/tests/aws/test_contest_orchestration_manifest.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/tests/aws/test_contest_runtime_cleanup.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/tests/aws/test_deploy_to_ec2_arch.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/tests/aws/test_orchestrate_arch_runtime.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/tests/aws/test_orchestrate_contests.py
- /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/tests/aws/test_orchestrate_remote_contract.py

What remains
- Decide migration end-state for legacy cleanup fallback (BrutusContest=true) and remove after old instances are fully rotated.
- Run one explicit operator smoke sequence to prove no cross-lane reuse with real AWS resources:
  - single path (make model-build-features-* && make model-train-remote)
  - multi path (task model:build-features:orchestration:dev && task aws:model:contests:run)
- Close child beads (5qjk, qyd4, 8vx9, 9r9v, boui) once reviewed, then close parent.

new_value: {"notes":"Root cause identified from /tmp/brutus-orchestrate-*.json: failures occur in run_training during round-robin sweep, with repeated SIGKILL/exit 137 (OOM) on .venv/bin/python -m predictor.pipeline.train_unified when using --max-competition-threads 2 --physical-cores 24 on c7gd.12xlarge. Recent runs (20260224051950/063640/065551) show error text truncated at 24017 chars ending with '--output truncated--', which hides final kill line and makes failure appear silent. Older runs in same pattern include explicit 'Killed' + 'code 137 (SIGKILL) / TimedOut mapping' in state error."}

{"notes":"Changed files:
- scripts/aws/orchestrate.py: added OOM-aware retry in _step_run_training (one retry max), helper detectors _is_probable_oom_failure and _reduce_training_parallelism, plus explicit OOM guidance in terminal errors.
- model/tests/aws/test_orchestrate_train_flags.py: added tests proving retry on exit_code=137 with reduced threads/cores and no retry for non-OOM failures.

{"notes":"- Removed retry-on-OOM behavior in scripts/aws/orchestrate.py (_step_run_training now hard-fails once; keeps explicit OOM hint).
- Added CloudWatch tail fallback in scripts/aws/run_model_remote_ssm.py so truncated SSM stderr includes final streamed tail (typically contains kill/OOM evidence).
- Added regression test model/tests/aws/test_run_model_remote_ssm.py.
- Verified with make model-sanity (pass: 1705 passed, 18 skipped, 5 deselected).
- Analysis: single-node and multi-node are not memory-identical. Shared train-unified memory fixes are present on single (c0dee62 venv), but multi-node shards reduce per-process target envelope (e.g. short shard split by window values), which lowers RSS versus single full sweep."}

{"notes":"HANDOFF CAPSULE

What changed
- Updated /Users/cartine/1brutus/scripts/aws/orchestrate.py to remove automatic retry-on-OOM in single-node orchestration; training now hard-fails once and returns an explicit probable-OOM hint.
- Updated /Users/cartine/1brutus/scripts/aws/run_model_remote_ssm.py to preserve/append CloudWatch streamed tail lines when SSM stderr is truncated, so terminal kill evidence remains visible in orchestration state errors.
- Added /Users/cartine/1brutus/model/tests/aws/test_run_model_remote_ssm.py to cover truncated-output tail recovery behavior.
- Confirmed with local state artifacts that single-node failures include true OOM signatures (exit 137/SIGKILL), while some recent runs were obscured by output truncation.
- Confirmed single-node vs multi-node memory envelope mismatch source: sharded multi-node target space is smaller per process than full single-node sweep.

What remains
- Re-run the failing single-node command and confirm the enhanced error payload now includes CloudWatch tail kill/OOM lines (no truncation blind spot).
- Decide whether single-node should keep full-sweep behavior or adopt a reduced/sharded sweep profile consistent with orchestration to cap RSS.
- If needed, add explicit RSS logging checkpoints to the non-contest/single-node path to mirror multi-node observability and compare peak memory stage-by-stage.
- Optionally add a preflight guard that warns/fails when computed target cardinality exceeds a configured per-instance memory envelope.

Verification steps
- make model-sanity (already run; pass: 1705 passed, 18 skipped, 5 deselected)."}

new_value: {"notes":"Implemented sweep-only unified training path (no no-sweep fallback) and removed manifest selected_* dependency for target reconstruction.\\n\\nWhat changed\\n- train_unified now rejects --skip-sweep immediately with hard failure.\\n- Removed no-sweep execution branch from train_unified main flow.\\n- Sweep winner targets are built only from in-memory sweep results (no reload/rebuild from settings.selected_*).\\n- _should_run_target_sweep now enforces sweep-only semantics (features dir required, enabled=true required).\\n- persistence_phase no longer reads settings.selected_long/settings.selected_short; it derives long/short thresholds from sweep winner target names and requires in-memory short winner metadata.\\n- Added Gate A metadata directly to SweepResult (gate_a_min_gap, gate_a_normalize_gap) and thread it from round_robin_finalize after gate-a evaluation.\\n\\nFiles\\n- model/src/predictor/pipeline/train_unified/__main__.py\\n- new_value: {"notes":"Root cause of no-arg contest failure identified and fixed in isolated multi-node path.\\n\\nProblem:\\n- eec81e5 moved contest default discovery to isolated S3 root (features/orchestration) and hard-failed when no prefixes existed.\\n- Existing no-arg workflow (build orchestration features locally, then run contests) had no automatic first upload into isolated root.\\n\\nFix (isolated path only):\\n- scripts/aws/contest_orchestration/manifest.py\\n  - resolve_features_s3_uri now auto-falls back to uploading latest local features into features/orchestration when isolated root is empty\\n  - added _auto_upload_local_orchestration_features helper using scripts/aws/upload_features.py\\n  - preserved hard guard blocking features/unified usage for contests\\n  - added dry_run propagation so --dry-run does not perform real uploads\\n- scripts/aws/orchestrate_contests.py\\n  - forwards dry_run flag into resolve_features_s3_uri\\n- model/tests/aws/test_contest_orchestration_manifest.py\\n  - added coverage for auto-upload fallback on empty isolated root\\n- docs/distributed-contests.md\\n  - documented zero-arg auto-upload behavior\\n\\nValidation:\\n- make model-sanity (PASS)\\n- smoke check: task aws:model:contests:run -- --dry-run (PASS, no-arg path auto-discovers local features and resolves isolated S3 URI)\\n"}

Implemented strict isolated multi-node features resolution (no fallback/failover):\n- scripts/aws/contest_orchestration/manifest.py: removed S3 prefix-scan path; resolve order is explicit URI -> manifest default -> run-id URI -> deterministic local auto-upload to features/orchestration.\n- Added hard invariant failure message when automatic upload source is missing/broken.\n- Enforced features URI must be s3:// and still rejects single-node features/unified namespace.\n- scripts/aws/orchestrate_contests.py: threaded dry-run into resolver so no-arg --dry-run uses the same deterministic path safely.\n- Updated tests in model/tests/aws/test_contest_orchestration_manifest.py for default auto-upload behavior and invariant failure behavior.\n- Updated docs/distributed-contests.md zero-arg behavior to reflect strict default + hard failure semantics.\n\nVerification:\n- make model-sanity (pass)\n- task aws:model:contests:run -- --dry-run (pass; zero-arg path auto-discovers local features and stages isolated upload URI)
Investigated failure run 20260225100034-56ae: all 4 shards failed with invariant error from orchestrate_remote_contest.py: missing worker command (no --worker-cmd and no BRUTUS_CONTEST_SHARD_COMMAND).\n\nFix implemented:\n- Added dedicated worker script scripts/aws/orchestrate_remote_contest_worker.py that executes one contest shard by seeding existing features URI into state, resuming remote orchestration, and syncing shard manifest after deploy before training.\n- Wired aws:model:train-remote-contest task to set BRUTUS_CONTEST_SHARD_COMMAND automatically to that worker script (no manual env var/flag required).\n- Added task precondition for worker script existence.\n- Added tests: model/tests/aws/test_orchestrate_remote_contest_worker.py\n- Updated docs: docs/distributed-contests.md + docs/multi-node-vs-single-node-flow.md to reflect auto-wired worker invariant.\n\nVerification:\n- make model-sanity (pass)\n- task aws:model:train-remote-contest -- --skip-upload-features --features-s3-uri ... --dry-run (pass; command now resolves to orchestrate_remote_contest_worker.py)\n- task aws:model:contests:run -- --dry-run (pass)
Note: ran  against 20260225100034-56ae to validate command path; this rewrote that run's local state/summary artifacts as succeeded (dry-run semantics). Not a git-tracked change."}

Investigated failure run 20260225100034-56ae: all 4 shards failed with invariant error from orchestrate_remote_contest.py: missing worker command (no --worker-cmd and no BRUTUS_CONTEST_SHARD_COMMAND).\n\nFix implemented:\n- Added dedicated worker script scripts/aws/orchestrate_remote_contest_worker.py that executes one contest shard by seeding existing features URI into state, resuming remote orchestration, and syncing shard manifest after deploy before training.\n- Wired aws:model:train-remote-contest task to set BRUTUS_CONTEST_SHARD_COMMAND automatically to that worker script (no manual env var/flag required).\n- Added task precondition for worker script existence.\n- Added tests: model/tests/aws/test_orchestrate_remote_contest_worker.py\n- Updated docs: docs/distributed-contests.md + docs/multi-node-vs-single-node-flow.md to reflect auto-wired worker invariant.\n\nVerification:\n- make model-sanity (pass)\n- task aws:model:train-remote-contest -- --skip-upload-features --features-s3-uri ... --dry-run (pass; command now resolves to orchestrate_remote_contest_worker.py)\n- task aws:model:contests:run -- --dry-run (pass)

Resolved multi-node catastrophic failure where all shards failed immediately. Root cause was missing argument forwarding in scripts/aws/orchestrate_remote_contest.py: _build_worker_command did not pass --skip-upload-features to worker processes, while worker enforces this invariant. Added forwarding and updated model/tests/aws/test_orchestrate_remote_contract.py to assert this flag is propagated. Revalidated aws:model:contests:run path: shard transitioned into remote training execution (no invariant failure). Performed contest cleanup and terminated launched instance i-0ea92fdf93d46121e."}

Resolved multi-node catastrophic failure where all shards failed immediately. Root cause was missing argument forwarding in scripts/aws/orchestrate_remote_contest.py: _build_worker_command did not pass --skip-upload-features to worker processes, while worker enforces this invariant. Added forwarding and updated model/tests/aws/test_orchestrate_remote_contract.py to assert this flag is propagated. Revalidated aws:model:contests:run path: shard transitioned into remote training execution (no invariant failure). Performed contest cleanup and terminated launched instance i-0ea92fdf93d46121e.
Removed remaining noop fallback in isolated shard runner. scripts/aws/orchestrate_remote_contest.py now fails fast before command construction when worker command is missing, and _build_worker_command no longer substitutes echo/noop. Updated contract tests to enforce dry-run invariant (worker required) and retain success case with explicit worker command. Verification: make model-sanity passed (1731 passed, 18 skipped, 5 deselected)."}

{"id":"brutus-yldj.3","title":"Short shards fail Gate A config invariant in distributed contests","description":"In distributed multi-node contest run 20260225112919-951d, both short shards fail with exit 201 due to missing required Gate A short sweep config lists.","notes":"Evidence:\\n- Run: model/data/results/orchestration/20260225112919-951d\\n- Failed shards: short-shard-001, short-shard-002\\n- Exit code: 201 for both shards\\n- Exception in shard stdout/stderr: GateAEvaluationError: missing or empty required config list(s): settings.sweep.full.short.min_gap_values, settings.sweep.full.short.normalize_gap_values.\\n\\nAdditional context:\\n- Long shards in same run succeeded; failure is short-path specific.\\n- Generated short shard manifest contains settings.short_min_gap and settings.short_normalize_gap, but not settings.sweep.full.short.min_gap_values or normalize_gap_values expected by Gate A evaluator.\\n\\nExpected behavior:\\n- Distributed short shard manifests include required Gate A config keys with non-empty values before launch.\\n- Orchestrator validates this invariant pre-launch and fails immediately with clear error if missing, rather than burning remote compute.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T11:48:25Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-25T11:48:25Z"}

{"id":"brutus-yldj.1","title":"Add real-time shard progress + host telemetry to contests run","description":"Distributed contest runs provide almost no runtime feedback. Operators cannot see launch progress, active node count, instance IDs, or shard health while the run is in progress.","notes":"Problem details:\\n- task aws:model:contests:run has long silent periods.\\n- No clear visibility into how many shard hosts launched.\\n- No surfaced mapping from shard to EC2 instance ID and run_id while in-flight.\\n- No periodic heartbeat/progress summary to show the orchestration is alive.\\n\\nExpected behavior:\\n- Orchestrator emits periodic progress updates during run.\\n- Progress includes shard counts by status (pending/running/succeeded/failed).\\n- Progress includes launched instance IDs and shard-to-host mapping as soon as available.\\n- Failures surface with shard ID, instance ID (if any), and last known step.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T11:45:05Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-25T11:45:05Z"}

{"id":"brutus-yldj.2","title":"Stop per-node Slack spam; aggregate node completion in coordinator output","description":"Individual contest nodes are emitting Slack messages directly, creating noisy and fragmented reporting. Node completion and result summaries should be centralized at the aggregator.","notes":"Problem details:\\n- Worker nodes send Slack independently.\\n- Slack output is noisy and difficult to interpret across shards.\\n- No single collated progress/result narrative from the orchestrator or aggregator.\\n\\nExpected behavior:\\n- Nodes do not send Slack directly in distributed contest mode.\\n- Aggregator/coordinator records per-node completion events and key outcome fields.\\n- Aggregator emits a human-readable consolidated distillation as nodes finish and a final collated summary when all nodes complete.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T11:45:10Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-25T11:45:10Z"}

{"id":"brutus-6ifu","title":"Real-time shard progress output in contest orchestration runtime","notes":"Add progress printing to _run_task and execute_pending_tasks in runtime.py. Add _elapsed_seconds helper.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T11:59:44Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-25T11:59:44Z"}

{"id":"brutus-0ezb","title":"Suppress per-node Slack and add consolidated Slack at coordinator level","notes":"Part A: append --no-slack-notify to worker commands in runtime.py. Part B: add Slack args and _notify_slack helper to orchestrate_contests.py.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T12:00:09Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-25T12:00:09Z"}

- Review/merge PR #890.
- Optionally add focused unit tests for winner_pair ranking tie-break semantics and synthesized winner CSV content.

Verification:
- make model-sanity (pass)

{"notes":"Handoff capsule (2026-03-02, session 2)

What changed (this session)
- Added signals-based period return computation to sweep_engine.py:
  _load_signals_parquet(), _apply_exit_strategy(), _compute_returns_from_signals()
- Wired _get_period_returns() into select_joint_winner_pair() inner loop
  with per-invocation caching for signal DataFrames and computed returns
- Added signals_parquet_path deserialization to _side_sweep_winner_from_payload()
  via new _optional_str() helper
- Moved _parse_strategy_key() earlier in inner loop (before returns lookup)
  to provide strategy_family/params to _get_period_returns()
- Added 3 new tests to test_multi_node_remote_training_runtime_joint_winner.py:
  - test_write_summary_joint_final_phase_uses_signals_parquet (end-to-end)
  - test_signals_based_computation_produces_deterministic_results
  - test_signals_parquet_path_survives_json_round_trip

Files changed
- model/src/predictor/pipeline/multi_node_remote_training/sweep_engine.py
- model/tests/aws/test_multi_node_remote_training_runtime_joint_winner.py

What remains
- Shard-side signal Parquet production: shard_sweep/runner.py needs to extract
  the signal DataFrame (predictions + 5-day horizon prices) from the sweep
  output and write it as Parquet per signal_contract.py. This requires modifying
  train_unified's sweep evaluator (_generate_signals in sweep_evaluator.py) to
  persist/return the signal DataFrame, since report_replay_v1.json only contains
  aggregated results, not signal-level data. The column names in train_unified
  (_h_entry_price, _h_high_d{1..5}, etc.) match signal_contract.py exactly.
- Once shards produce signal Parquets, the orchestrator will automatically use
  them (the _get_period_returns function checks signals_parquet_path and routes
  to the new signals-based computation when available).
- The strategy_period_returns path remains as the active path until shards
  produce signal Parquets. No code removal needed until migration is complete.

Verification
- make model-sanity (1751 passed, 18 skipped, 5 deselected, lint/format/typecheck clean)
- Commit: 5252faa

  2. Delegates to PnlRollEvaluator._generate_signals() for threshold/top-K
  3. Subsets output to signal_contract columns and validates

{"notes":"## HARD SCOPE INVARIANT (NEVER VIOLATE)

**multi_node_remote_training (and ANY code serving it, including shard_sweep) MUST NOT import, reference, touch, or depend on train_unified or single_node_remote — EVER.**

- No imports (static or dynamic via importlib)
- No string-concatenation tricks to bypass guardrails
- No "bridge modules" that live outside multi_node_remote_training but import train_unified
- No modifications to train_unified files for multi_node purposes
- The boundary is DATA (JSON, Parquet artifacts), not code
- If you need functionality from train_unified, COPY/PASTE it into multi_node_remote_training
- The shard runner template in shard_worker.py runs on EC2 and CAN use train_unified (it's a generated script, not an import in the orchestrator)

## Violations discovered and reverted (session 4, 2026-03-02)

### Violation 1: shard_sweep/ module imported train_unified
- Created predictor.pipeline.shard_sweep as a separate "bridge module"
- runner.py line 166: importlib.import_module("predictor.pipeline.train_unified.__main__")
- runner.py line 286: from predictor.pipeline.train_unified.pnl_roll.sweep_evaluator import PnlRollEvaluator
- __init__.py docstring falsely claimed "IS allowed to import train_unified"
- REVERTED: shard_sweep/ deleted entirely

### Violation 2: train_unified files modified for multi_node purposes
- pnl_roll/types.py: added signals_df field to PnlRollResult
- pnl_roll/sweep_evaluator.py: added signals_df= to 4 PnlRollResult constructors
- round_robin_sweep.py: changed _evaluate_fold_algos return type, added signal capture logic
- REVERTED: all uncommitted train_unified changes restored via git checkout

### Violation 3: pre-existing violation on main branch
- sweep_engine.py:393: module_name = "predictor.pipeline." + "train_unified.__main__" (string-concatenation to bypass guardrails regex)
- This was the ORIGINAL violation that the bead was supposed to fix
- FIXED: run_side_sweep(), _load_train_main_module(), and all train_unified-dependent code removed from sweep_engine.py
- Shard execution logic moved to shard_worker.py template string (runs on EC2, not orchestrator)

### Guardrails hardened
- Added model/src/.../multi_node_remote_training/ to guardrails scan targets (was missing)
- Added string-concatenation detection pattern: ["']train_unified
- Added guardrails:shard-template tag for EC2 template exemptions

## Handoff capsule (session 4, 2026-03-02)

### THIS ITERATION WAS NULLIFIED
The entire session 3 implementation was reverted because it violated the hard scope invariant by:
1. Creating shard_sweep/ which imported from train_unified
2. Modifying train_unified files (types.py, sweep_evaluator.py, round_robin_sweep.py)
3. Building on the pre-existing main-branch violation instead of fixing it

The state is now PRIOR to session 3's work, with the additional fix of removing the pre-existing main-branch violation.

### What changed (this session — session 4)
- Reverted commits f5f84bf and b4bcf09 (shard_sweep creation and signal extraction)
- Removed run_side_sweep, run_sweep_and_build_targets, run_target_sweep, _load_train_main_module from sweep_engine.py
- Moved shard execution into shard_worker.py template (self-contained on EC2)
- Hardened guardrails to scan model path and catch string-concat bypasses
- Reverted all uncommitted train_unified modifications

### What remains
- Signal Parquet production for ALL candidates (not just winner) — must be implemented WITHOUT touching train_unified
- The approach must use only DATA artifacts (JSON, Parquet) crossing the shard/orchestrator boundary
- Any signal generation logic needed by multi_node must be copy/pasted into multi_node_remote_training, never imported
- Tests for the shard worker template need to be added/updated
- make model-sanity NOT YET RUN (structural changes only, no logic changes to train_unified)

### Commits
- 9dd7652: revert shard_sweep module and train_unified violations
- ac1de3d: remove all train_unified imports from multi_node_remote_training

### Verification needed
- make model-sanity (to verify no regressions from removing run_side_sweep from sweep_engine.py)"}

{"notes":"## Handoff Capsule (Session 4 — failure investigation)

### Root Cause: All 4 EC2 shards crash on run 20260303121902-b003

The new `_SIDE_SWEEP_RUNNER_TEMPLATE` (introduced in e15fbdb/dc58931) reads manifest and features paths from environment variables with WRONG defaults:

- `BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH` defaults to `/opt/brutus/manifest.toml`
- `BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR` defaults to `/opt/brutus/features`

But the actual paths on the EC2 instance are:

- Manifest: `$WORK_ROOT/model/data/manifest.toml` (placed by `_sync_manifest`)
- Features: `$WORK_ROOT/model/data/unified/<dir_name>/` (placed by `_deploy_code`)

Where `$WORK_ROOT` is `/mnt/nvme/brutus` on NVMe instances (m7gd) or `/opt/brutus` on non-NVMe (c7i).

Even on c7i where WORK_ROOT=/opt/brutus, the paths are wrong:
- Expected: /opt/brutus/model/data/manifest.toml → Default: /opt/brutus/manifest.toml (WRONG)
- Expected: /opt/brutus/model/data/unified/<dir>/ → Default: /opt/brutus/features (WRONG)

The training bash script (`_build_training_script` in shard_worker.py:945-1021) does NOT export `BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH` or `BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR`, so the Python runner always uses the wrong defaults.

### Fix required

In `_build_training_script()` (shard_worker.py ~line 987), add these exports after the SHELL_RESOLVE_SNIPPET:

```bash
export BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH=$WORK_ROOT/model/data/manifest.toml
export BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR=$WORK_ROOT/model/data/unified/<dir_name>
```

Where `<dir_name>` is derived from `features_s3_uri.rstrip('/').split('/')[-1]` (same as in `_build_deployment_commands`).

### Evidence
- Run ID: 20260303121902-b003 — all 4 shards failed with `exit_code=1`
- Previous run (20260302085547-16da) succeeded using old template that called `run_sweep_and_build_targets()` which used relative `data/manifest.toml` from cwd
- Venv at dc58931 was correctly deployed (confirmed via S3 latest-*.txt timestamps)
- Features exist at S3 URI (features.parquet, manifest.toml, ticker_encoder.pkl all present)

### What remains
1. Fix `_build_training_script()` to export BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH and BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR
2. Re-run the 4-shard sweep to validate the fix
3. Tests for shard_sweep pnl_roll exit strategy modules (out-of-scope for this bead)

### Verification
- `make model-sanity` for code changes
- Re-run end-to-end 4-shard sweep to confirm fix"}

{"notes":"## Handoff Capsule (Session 4, segment 2 — second failure investigation)

### Run 20260303150327-8e74: All 4 shards failed again

The env var fix (af4d91d) was necessary but insufficient. There is a SECOND bug in the runner template.

### Bug 1 (FIXED in af4d91d): Missing env var exports
`_build_training_script()` did not export `BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH` or `BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR`.
**Status**: Fixed and pushed.

### Bug 2 (NOT FIXED): `load_manifest()` return value mismatch
**File**: `scripts/aws/multi_node_remote_training/shard_worker.py`, line 131 (inside `_SIDE_SWEEP_RUNNER_TEMPLATE`)

```python
manifest, manifest_settings = load_manifest(manifest_path)  # CRASHES
```

`load_manifest()` returns `Manifest | None` — a single object, NOT a tuple.
This line crashes with `TypeError: cannot unpack non-iterable Manifest` on EVERY shard.

**Fix**: Replace the one-liner with the correct two-step pattern:
```python
from predictor.manifest import load_manifest, resolve_settings

manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
assert manifest is not None, (
    f'Failed invariant: manifest not found at {manifest_path}'
)
manifest_settings = resolve_settings(manifest.settings, environment='remote')
```

This also requires adding `resolve_settings` to the import line at the top of the template (line 65).

### Additional issue: BRUTUS_EXECUTION_DIR not exported
Line 168 inside the template:
```python
execution_dir = Path(os.environ.get('BRUTUS_EXECUTION_DIR', '/tmp/brutus-execution'))
```
This default (`/tmp/brutus-execution`) likely won't match the actual execution dir created by `ShardSweepConfig.get_execution_dir()`, which writes under `features_dir/<execution_id>`. This will cause the `report_replay_v1.json` check at line 170-173 to fail even after the sweep succeeds. Either:
- Export `BRUTUS_EXECUTION_DIR` in the bash wrapper, OR
- Derive execution_dir from the sweep result rather than an env var

### Summary of all 3 fixes needed
1. ~~Export BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH and BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR~~ (done, af4d91d)
2. Fix `load_manifest()` return value unpacking + add `resolve_settings` call
3. Fix BRUTUS_EXECUTION_DIR or derive execution_dir from sweep result

### Verification
- `make model-sanity` for code changes
- Re-run 4-shard sweep end-to-end"}

The shard must also produce signal Parquet artifacts per signal_contract.py so the orchestrator can do confidence-ordered joint execution.","notes":"## Handoff Capsule (Session 4 — failure investigation)

### Root Cause: All 4 EC2 shards crash on run 20260303121902-b003

The new `_SIDE_SWEEP_RUNNER_TEMPLATE` (introduced in e15fbdb/dc58931) reads manifest and features paths from environment variables with WRONG defaults:

- `BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH` defaults to `/opt/brutus/manifest.toml`
- `BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR` defaults to `/opt/brutus/features`

But the actual paths on the EC2 instance are:

- Manifest: `$WORK_ROOT/model/data/manifest.toml` (placed by `_sync_manifest`)
- Features: `$WORK_ROOT/model/data/unified/<dir_name>/` (placed by `_deploy_code`)

Where `$WORK_ROOT` is `/mnt/nvme/brutus` on NVMe instances (m7gd) or `/opt/brutus` on non-NVMe (c7i).

Even on c7i where WORK_ROOT=/opt/brutus, the paths are wrong:
- Expected: /opt/brutus/model/data/manifest.toml → Default: /opt/brutus/manifest.toml (WRONG)
- Expected: /opt/brutus/model/data/unified/<dir>/ → Default: /opt/brutus/features (WRONG)

The training bash script (`_build_training_script` in shard_worker.py:945-1021) does NOT export `BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH` or `BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR`, so the Python runner always uses the wrong defaults.

### Fix required

In `_build_training_script()` (shard_worker.py ~line 987), add these exports after the SHELL_RESOLVE_SNIPPET:

```bash
export BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH=$WORK_ROOT/model/data/manifest.toml
export BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR=$WORK_ROOT/model/data/unified/<dir_name>
```

Where `<dir_name>` is derived from `features_s3_uri.rstrip('/').split('/')[-1]` (same as in `_build_deployment_commands`).

### Evidence
- Run ID: 20260303121902-b003 — all 4 shards failed with `exit_code=1`
- Previous run (20260302085547-16da) succeeded using old template that called `run_sweep_and_build_targets()` which used relative `data/manifest.toml` from cwd
- Venv at dc58931 was correctly deployed (confirmed via S3 latest-*.txt timestamps)
- Features exist at S3 URI (features.parquet, manifest.toml, ticker_encoder.pkl all present)

### What remains
1. Fix `_build_training_script()` to export BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH and BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR
2. Re-run the 4-shard sweep to validate the fix
3. Tests for shard_sweep pnl_roll exit strategy modules (out-of-scope for this bead)

### Verification
- `make model-sanity` for code changes

The shard must also produce signal Parquet artifacts per signal_contract.py so the orchestrator can do confidence-ordered joint execution.","notes":"## Handoff Capsule (Session 4, segment 2 — second failure investigation)

### Run 20260303150327-8e74: All 4 shards failed again

The env var fix (af4d91d) was necessary but insufficient. There is a SECOND bug in the runner template.

### Bug 1 (FIXED in af4d91d): Missing env var exports
`_build_training_script()` did not export `BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH` or `BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR`.
**Status**: Fixed and pushed.

### Bug 2 (NOT FIXED): `load_manifest()` return value mismatch
**File**: `scripts/aws/multi_node_remote_training/shard_worker.py`, line 131 (inside `_SIDE_SWEEP_RUNNER_TEMPLATE`)

```python
manifest, manifest_settings = load_manifest(manifest_path)  # CRASHES
```

`load_manifest()` returns `Manifest | None` — a single object, NOT a tuple.
This line crashes with `TypeError: cannot unpack non-iterable Manifest` on EVERY shard.

**Fix**: Replace the one-liner with the correct two-step pattern:
```python
from predictor.manifest import load_manifest, resolve_settings

manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
assert manifest is not None, (
    f'Failed invariant: manifest not found at {manifest_path}'
)
manifest_settings = resolve_settings(manifest.settings, environment='remote')
```

This also requires adding `resolve_settings` to the import line at the top of the template (line 65).

### Additional issue: BRUTUS_EXECUTION_DIR not exported
Line 168 inside the template:
```python
execution_dir = Path(os.environ.get('BRUTUS_EXECUTION_DIR', '/tmp/brutus-execution'))
```
This default (`/tmp/brutus-execution`) likely won't match the actual execution dir created by `ShardSweepConfig.get_execution_dir()`, which writes under `features_dir/<execution_id>`. This will cause the `report_replay_v1.json` check at line 170-173 to fail even after the sweep succeeds. Either:
- Export `BRUTUS_EXECUTION_DIR` in the bash wrapper, OR
- Derive execution_dir from the sweep result rather than an env var

### Summary of all 3 fixes needed
1. ~~Export BRUTUS_MANIFEST_PATH and BRUTUS_FEATURES_DIR~~ (done, af4d91d)
2. Fix `load_manifest()` return value unpacking + add `resolve_settings` call
3. Fix BRUTUS_EXECUTION_DIR or derive execution_dir from sweep result

### Verification
- `make model-sanity` for code changes
- Re-run 4-shard sweep end-to-end","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T08:46:37Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-03-03T15:44:20Z"}

{"description":"Three bugs in planner.py host allocation:

1. aggregation_host (+1) is never used for remote execution — remove the +1 from expected_hosts (line 36), remove aggregation_host extraction (line 78), remove from OrchestrationState in runtime.py, remove from create_runtime_state params, and update build_shard_plan return type.

2. Host validation requires one [[hosts]] entry per shard slot (expected_hosts = 2*n), but hosts have max_parallel which means fewer entries can serve more shards. E.g. 4 hosts with max_parallel=2 = 8 slots. Validation should check sum(h.max_parallel for h in hosts) >= 2*shards_per_side.

3. Host-to-shard assignment (lines 76-77) does naive slicing (hosts[:n], hosts[n:2n]) which only works with 1:1 host:shard. Needs to expand/round-robin hosts respecting max_parallel to fill 2*n shard slots.

Files to change: planner.py, runtime.py, manifest.py (if HostSpec needs changes).","title":"Fix host allocation in multi-node orchestrator: remove aggregation_host, support max_parallel"}

- **scripts/aws/multi_node_remote_training/runtime.py**:
  - Added `arch: str | None = None` field to TaskRecord

### Root cause
Shard workers that couldn't find a pre-existing EC2 instance needed to create one. `create_ml_instance` requires architecture (x86_64 vs arm64) but the runner never passed --arch. Hit: `RuntimeError('Failed invariant: --arch unresolved before launch.')`

{"id":"brutus-fvr","title":"Docker-native deploy of unified trainer to EC2","notes":"Replace the current venv-tarball-extraction deployment with running the Docker image directly on EC2. Current approach builds a Docker image locally (Dockerfile.ec2 on amazonlinux:2023), extracts a venv tarball, uploads to S3, then untars on bare EC2. This defeats Docker's reproducibility guarantee — the exact image that passed smoke tests locally is not what runs on EC2.

Target: push the Docker image to ECR, pull on EC2, run with bind-mounted NVMe storage. Same image locally and remotely. No tarball extraction, no hoping system libs match.

Benefits:
- Exact same runtime environment locally and on EC2 (Docker's core value prop)
- Eliminates fragile tarball-extraction deployment step
- Eliminates dependency on matching system libraries between Docker build env and EC2 AMI
- Simplifies deployment to: pull image + docker run
- SSM commands become `docker run` invocations instead of bare-metal shell scripts","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-08T15:53:51Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-17T09:32:18Z"}

{"id":"brutus-664m","title":"Fix missing --arch in shard task command","description":"runtime._build_task_command does not pass --arch to the shard entrypoint. When a shard worker needs to create a new EC2 instance, it fails with 'Failed invariant: --arch unresolved before launch'.","notes":"## Handoff Capsule

### What changed
- **scripts/aws/multi_node_remote_training/runtime.py**:
  - Added `arch: str | None = None` field to TaskRecord
  - Added `_resolve_task_arches()` function that resolves instance_type → venv arch via EC2 DescribeInstanceTypes API (reuses existing `_resolve_instance_type_venv_arches`)
  - Called in `execute_pending_tasks` after venv prebuild, before launching ThreadPoolExecutor
  - `_build_task_command` now includes `--arch <arch>` when task.arch is set
  - Skipped in dry_run mode (no AWS calls needed)
- **model/tests/aws/test_multi_node_remote_training_runtime_execution.py**: Added `test_build_task_command_includes_arch_when_resolved` test

### Root cause
Shard workers that couldn't find a pre-existing EC2 instance needed to create one. `create_ml_instance` requires architecture (x86_64 vs arm64) but the runner never passed --arch. Hit: `RuntimeError('Failed invariant: --arch unresolved before launch.')`

### What remains
- [ ] Run `make model-sanity` to verify lint/typecheck/tests pass
- [ ] Verify real run: all 8 shards get --arch in their commands and create instances successfully
- [ ] Consider whether find_ml_instance race condition needs addressing (two shards with same instance_type could claim same existing instance)

### Verification
```
make model-sanity
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T13:56:49Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-03-04T14:21:24Z"}

{"id":"brutus-13ms","title":"Skip venv upload when tarball hash already exists in S3","description":"Implement hash/sum-based dedupe for venv uploads so aws:model:upload-venv skips uploading tarballs already present in S3 for the same env+arch while preserving pointer consistency and clear logging.","notes":"Add content-hash based dedupe for aws:model:upload-venv so uploads are skipped when the same venv tarball is already present in S3 for the target env/arch.

Scope:
- Compute stable hash/sum for local venv tarball (or source-derived canonical hash if stronger).
- Check S3 for existing object/manifest keyed by that hash.
- If found, skip upload and update latest pointer metadata safely.
- Keep behavior arch-aware (amd64/arm64) and env-aware (dev/prod).
- Ensure orchestration prebuild/upload path can consume this without race issues.

Acceptance criteria:
- Re-running upload with unchanged venv performs no data transfer and exits success.
- Changed venv content results in a new upload.
- Logging clearly states skipped vs uploaded and which hash was used.
- Tests cover both hit/miss cases and pointer update behavior.

Note: issue requested only; implementation should not be executed in this session.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"cartine@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-20T09:34:24Z","created_by":"Andrew Cartine","updated_at":"2026-02-20T09:34:32Z"}
